### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-16T05:30:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashimotos-experts.1475/post-24501>

Bren, I'm sorry but your thyroid numbers are not optimal. Your FT3 should be in the top of the range although the FT4 looks OK but could climb a tad. This means you could be taking a little more of the Armour. Make sure you shew it or crunch it. The latest version of Armour has some fillers that need some breaking apart so your body can get to the good stuff. I usually take NP Thyroid by Acella (generic version of Armour) and it just dissolves almost immediately and works great! Try going to [www.stopthethyroidmadness.com](http://http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com) for good ideas on how to raise your dose. I followed those instructions and I felt wonderful. Also watch your thyroid numbers as you raise your dose. If you FT3 continues to be sluggish and FT4 gets higher you may be a poor converter (of T4 to FT3).

I guess it's time to start icing the carotid. I'm currently icing the jaw to prep for a minor surgery so might as well add the carotid now too! New task for all us Hashis folks!!

### ColdBren — 2012-05-16T05:35:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashimotos-experts.1475/post-24504>

Thanks Patty, I am so new to the whole thyroid thing...I thought my issue was something else as my docs always said my thyroid was okay. I am so bummed. Thanks for the website tip too, looks good. I will start icing right away, and dealing with my gut.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-05-16T07:18:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashimotos-experts.1475/post-24525>

This was from Dr K's Leaky Gut Rx blog post... thought it may be helpful to you, Bren. Lauren

The Hashimoto’s thing……it makes up 95% of all hypothyroid human cases today and it treated by medicine with CW. Most who have it have leaky guts and low levels of intracellular glutathione. Glutathione is a very important antioxidant that plays an important role in maintaining the correct oxidation-reduction potential inside cells. It scavenges oxidising free radicals, detoxifies heavy metals, pesticides, tylenol and leukotrienes. It stores and transports amino acids, regulates the cell cycle, protein synthesis and gene expression, and protects thyroid cells from self-generated hydrogen peroxide. Glutathione is quite important for patients with Hashimoto’s. Glutathione protects the body in an extraordinary number of ways, but the fact that it protects the thyroid against hydrogen peroxide is particularly significant for the thyroid. In untreated Hashimoto’s, the body’s levels of T4 (thyroxine) begin to fall, and TSH (thyroid stimulating hormone) levels generally start to climb. In order to stimulate the thyroid to make more T4, the TSH activates the thyroid cells to make hydrogen peroxide. If the TSH levels remain high, the thyroid cells continue to produce more and more hydrogen peroxide, and this can lead to increased inflammation, scarring, and ultimately the destruction of thyroid cells. If the hydrogen peroxide manages to gain entry to the cell, the damage becomes far worse.

Hydrogen peroxide is thought to play a significant role in the development and progression of Hashimoto’s disease, despite the fact that the thyroid gland actually requires hydrogen peroxide for thyroid hormone formation. Hydrogen peroxide is normally produced during the oxidation of the iodine ions. It is an essential part of the thyroid function. But the critical factor is the protection of the thyroid cells (glutathione level), and the place in which the iodine ions are oxidized. If oxidation occurs inside the cells, the hydrogen peroxide is produced inside the cells, and the cells sustain damage.

A study found that if hydrogen peroxide is allowed to enter thyroid cells, it attacks and cleaves thyroglobulin (protein within the thyroid), producing fragments that are able to diffuse into other cells – and these fragments were recognized by autoantibodies taken from people with Hashimoto’s disease. This suggests that hydrogen peroxide entry into thyroid cells may actually be the cause of Hashimoto’s disease!!!! SO if you have Hashimoto’s your glutathione levels maybe a proxy of how bad your disease really is. It can be a measure of really how leaky your gut is too.

The predominant infiltrating cells in autoimmune diseases such as Hashimoto’s thyroiditis are phagocytic macrophages, neutrophils and various T-cells; the macrophages and neutrophils damage the tissues by releasing inflammatory cytokines and proteins, including hydrogen peroxide making things worse because this further depletes glutathione inside the cells. This is complicated by co-morbid B12 and folate deficiencies in the diet because of the leaky gut. Glutathione requires both to be on hand to work well!

The body’s stores of glutathione can become depleted through any stressor. For example, alcoholism, HIV infection, cirrhosis, diabetes, surgical trauma, fasting, environmental toxins, overuse of acetaminophen, chronic stress, inadequate diet, elevated adrenaline release, extensive strenuous exercise, or infection. It is also associated with cigarette smoking, as smoking increases the rate at which the body uses glutathione.

Glutathione is not easily absorbed through oral supplementation, but is synthesized in the body from amino acids, and body levels can be increased through oral administration of glutathione precursors such as N-acetylcysteine (NAC), S-adenosyl-L-methionine (SAMe) and L-gluatmine. Getting it into cells is even tougher and requires IV infusions using lipid soluble mediums. For serious autoimmune diseases it is often a great treatment. Oral administration of NAC of doses up to 8,000 mg/day was not found to cause clinically significant adverse reactions, although doses of 600mg/day were enough to significantly improve symptoms of chronic lung disease like COPD. I have used over 3000 mgs a day when my HS-CRP was around 3.0. When I have falls in Vitamin D levels I also reach for NAC or sometimesWHEY protein. Whey also breaks down into glutathione and reduces inflammation. This is why Whey use can augment weight loss in people with high CRP’s

Intake of NAC does increase zinc loss through urine, so zinc supplementation would be advisable for anyone supplementing NAC over an extended period. This can deplete your sex steroid hormones if you do not eat a lot of foods with zinc. Me personally when I take a ton I always make my oysters rockerfeller recipe.

Glutathione can also be up-regulated by vitamins C and E, and alpha lipoic acid, PQQ, spinach, offal, yeast extract, and broccoli. N-acetylcysteine is considered to be the most effective, fastest route to raising levels of glutathione. It also increases levels of glutathione only when there is an actual need, and it seems to concentrate only in tissues where glutathione is required. This need corresponds to high MMP9 levels in cells.

The fact that NAC is able to restore glutathione levels and thereby protect the thyroid is particularly interesting given its ability to crack open bacteria such as chlamydia pneumonia, which hide inside the body’s own cells during one part of their life cycle, using the host cell’s machinery to replicate. Chlamydia pneumoniae has been implicated in autoimmune diseases such as multiple sclerosis, and N-acetylcysteine is considered an important part of treatment. I do not consider these diseases as part of AI’s. I consider finding titers of these in patients to be patho-mneumonic that glutathione levels are very very low in these people.

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-05-21T19:14:43+0300

> **Jennifer;24895 wrote:** I'm no expert, but in my experience the short fuse is either an adrenal thing (low blood sugar) or a no estrogen thing. I don't think DHEA would make you more aggressive. Someone else chime in if I"m off base here...

ha, and get used to doc's telling you your thyroid numbers look great when in fact they dont. unfortunately you have to educate yourself and get help elsewhere (i'd find a new doc if it were me)

i agree with this...i sometimes feel worse in the afternoon bc my adrenals can't keep up with the thyroid medication. I'm not on a super low dose of hydrocortisone (15mg)...i was on adaptogens before that and they may be enough to work for you. Apex Energetics makes something called "adaptocrin" that is pretty good. Licorice root tea can help keep cortisol in your system longer. The stop the thyroid website is a great resource and actually mentions this in their dosing instructions - how not being on enough or ramping up quickly enough can cause rebound affects.

I agree you need more thyroid meds. your FT3 is esp in the toilet. Be sure to ask for RT3 as you continue to test bc you want to ensure your T4 is converting to T3 and not RT3. If you have RT3, take milk thistle extract to help support your liver in clearing it.

you may be able to get your antibodies to "disappear" on the tests but unfortunately that does not mean you'll be cured (plus sometimes you are still producing anitbodies but they just dont measure them below a certain point...mine only shows >30, so if you are scoring 29, you'll look like you arent producing any which is incorrect). Once your body produces anitbodies - it never stops. You are lucky to catch this early though bc you can def minimize the effects and hopefully not have it be an all out battle!

other tips

i take NAC to help my gut and thyroid, and i also started LDN (low dose naltrexone - an immune system modulator)

avoid inflammatory foods - nightshades, grains, eggs or at least the ones that seem to affect you

# Thread: How much protein for lunch and supper?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### justjoan060 — 2012-05-15T19:05:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24261>

I get about the BAB, but have only seen sketchy "meat and veggies" information about the other two meals. I'm wondering how much protein I should be shooting for the other two meals to keep hunger away. I'm sure there's a thread somewhere, but can't find it. What do the rest of you do?

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-15T20:22:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24305>

However much you need to eat to end your hunger.

I know that sound like a smart-alec reply but I don't mean it that way. I don't count, weight or measure. I eat. If I'm full I stop. If I'm still hungry I eat some more.

I have a lifetime of trust issues to overcome but I am slowly learning to just trust my body. Listen to it. Learn from it. Now that I'm no longer trying to control it all that is a bunch easier.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-15T22:16:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24335>

I'm trying to get at least 20-25 carbs a day in, I'm a little afraid to go too low. What does Jack say about that? I love mixed salads. But it can take a lot of salad to get very many carbs in, plus then I'm hungry. The obvious thing would be to go to different vegetables. I steamed some broccoli last night and have fresh green beans in the fridge.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-15T22:57:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24360>

Why are you afraid of getting too few carbs? I read somewhere that there's no such thing as an essential carb. And if I understand what Jack said (hahahahaha, that's pretty funny. Me understanding what he says, I mean) in the dead of winter he goes totally zero carb for several weeks. He's eating seasonally if I read that correctly.

I'd suggest if your energy starts to really lag you might need to add a bit more carbs to your diet. If you're doing the leptin reset then your breakfast should have zero (or as close as you can get) carbs.

I think of paleo as Atkins on steroids. Especially for those of us who need to lose weight. The fewer carbs the better. Again, learning to listen to your body will help. If you start craving carbs then maybe you need to add a few. I'm eating lots of sweet potatoes lately. (that means about 1/2 a cup a day ... that's MY definition of a lot, lol). Jack recommended this book "Sweet Potato Power Discover Your Personal Equation for Optimal Health" which, of course, I promptly ran off to Amazon and d/l to my Kindle. I'm such a groupie.

Hope all that helps a little.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-15T23:53:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24381>

Don't forget the fat. I eat two tblsp of coconut oil with almost each meal. I find that helps curb any hunger and I can go 5-6 hours between meals. For me, more protein was not better but more fat was! Maybe a salad, 3 oz of a protein and then some extra fat will help keep you full.

### nonchalant — 2012-05-16T00:16:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24393>

I think you should control your hunger with enough protein and fat. Then when that is under control, begin to eat the carbs.

If you use up too much of your tummy space on carbs, you might not be getting enough protein and fat to be satisfied. Carbs are tricky, and you have to be careful with them. Protein and fat are self-limiting, if you're not being urged on by other carbs. Be sure to drizzle good butter over those veggies! I always eat the meat first now, then fill in with whatever veggies are appealing, until it's time to stop.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-16T23:42:10+0300

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-17T02:48:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24745>

Joan I didn't gain any weight when I started on the hormones. But remember I'm taking HUGE doses in a rhythmic pattern. The Wiley Protocol is meant to mimic your hormone production from when you were in your 20's. Most CW docs would pass out if they knew this.

### tes3@mac.com — 2012-05-17T16:42:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24878>

# Thread: Estrogen and the gut, candida too (can't the odd carb eating carnivore get a break?)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/estrogen-and-the-gut-candida-too-cant-the-odd-carb-eating-carnivore-get-a-break.1478/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-15T20:14:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/estrogen-and-the-gut-candida-too-cant-the-odd-carb-eating-carnivore-get-a-break.1478/post-24298>

[http://joe.endocrinology-journals.org/content/190/3/749.full.pdf](http://http://joe.endocrinology-journals.org/content/190/3/749.full.pdf)

Gastric estrogen directly induces ghrelin expression and production

in the rat stomach

Abstract

Ghrelin, an endogenous ligand for the GH secretagogue

receptor, is predominantly produced in the stomach. Little is

known about the regulation mechanism of gastric ghrelin.

**Here, we report that estrogen synthesized in the stomach

induces rat gastric ghrelin gene expression and production.We

established a gastric ghrelin cell enrichment method using

Percoll centrifugation and then studied the effect of estrogen

and/or its antagonist on ghrelin expression and production.

Treatment with estrogen for 8 h significantly increased the

**level of ghrelin expression, and ICI-182 780, an estrogen

receptor (ER) antagonist, completely reversed this effect.**

Reverse transcriptase-PCR analysis clearly showed that ERa

and aromatase are expressed in the female rat stomach.

Moreover, treatment with an aromatase inhibitor, 4-hydroxyandrostenedione

(formestane), significantly decreased the

level of ghrelin mRNA expression in minced stomach tissue.

In vivo studies revealed that the ghrelin mRNA expression and

production did not change in gonadectomized rat 3weeks after

surgery. These results strongly suggest that estrogen produced

in the stomach directly induces ghrelin expression and

production in both female and male rat stomachs.**

Journal of Endocrinology (2006) 190, 749–757

### chocolate — 2012-05-15T20:18:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/estrogen-and-the-gut-candida-too-cant-the-odd-carb-eating-carnivore-get-a-break.1478/post-24303>

[http://www.yeastinfectionadvisor.com/recurringyeastinfection.html](http://http://www.yeastinfectionadvisor.com/recurringyeastinfection.html)

Recurring Yeast Infection and How to Get Rid of It For Good

If you get a recurring yeast infection at certain times of the month you are not alone. Many women get them right at the start of ovulation and they last until menstruation, and then go away only to return again at ovulation the next month. Other women get them at other times of their cycle but this is not as common. If this is you, then this treatment plan will solve the problem in the least amount of time.

**In a scientific study done in 1999 by Paul L. Fidel Jr., Jessica Cutright, and Chad Steele of Louisiana State University, and published in the American Society of Microbiology in 2000, they found that progesterone has no effect on vaginal candida in mice but excess estrogen promoted its growth. They also found that excess estrogen reduced the ability of skin cells to prevent colonization of candida on the vaginal walls.

Estrogen is responsible for glycogen levels in the vagina and glycogen feeds yeast. Excessive levels have been linked to breast cancer and reduced function of the Thymic gland. The Thymic gland helps immune system t-cells that are grown in the bone marrow mature. At a certain stage of t-cell growth in the bone marrow the t-cells migrate to the thymic gland to mature. Upon maturity of the t-cells, the thymic gland releases them into the body.**

Because recurring yeast infection becomes hormone dependent in the vagina and feeds on imbalances of hormones or the sugar produced by excess estrogen, you should test your hormone levels to determine what they are or you could just treat yourself for excess estrogen, its your choice. The most accurate test is by saliva testing. This test can be done in the comfort of your own home by mail and I recommend the test below.

A 5 Panel Saliva Hormone Test is the best way to test for hormone imbalances related to recurring yeast infections.

What is the evolutionary purpose for yeast, to give b- vitamins to bring the metals back to the liver for redistribution?

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-15T20:46:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/estrogen-and-the-gut-candida-too-cant-the-odd-carb-eating-carnivore-get-a-break.1478/post-24319>

### Steven — 2012-05-15T22:37:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story.1480/post-24346>

Hello,

I'm a 28 year old male.

Since the age of 15, I have used a computer everyday for at least 3+ hours per day and stayed up late until 2am. I also ate a diet high in carbohydrates everyday. Even in college I maintained this kind of life style. However, I'm not over weight though. My mom is always yelling at me to sleep earlier....

I didn't pay much attention until a year ago when I developed adrenal fatigue that is causing symptoms such as palpitations, dizziness, ringing in the ears, and cold hands + feet.

I would like to try Dr. Kruse's protocol to see if I can improve my health and get rid of these symptoms so I can live a normal life again!

Thanks for reading.

### nonchalant — 2012-05-15T22:53:37+0300

### Steven — 2012-05-18T21:04:11+0300

Hi Nonchalant, will look into f.lux.

Thanks for the suggestion!

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-20T18:56:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-story.1480/post-25733>

Good luck Steven. I also get the palpitations etc and observing a good sleep schedule, eating a keto paleo diet (per the leptin rx), and especially tightening up on use of artificial light after sunset, is a tremendous help. Also CT. The more you read here the more you realize our American diet and high tech lifestyle has created biological mismatches that are seriously affecting our health, individually and collectively. And our medical wisdom will prescribe us drugs to treat the symptoms which have side effects of their own....

Here you will learn how you got the way you are and how to fix yourself. If you can get some labs done that will really help to understand what is going on. Read up anything JK has written on adrenal fatigue and leaky gut (surprise! They are related!) .... The good news is.... This can be fixed! It will require you to do some things differently though

Speaking for myself, my health has improved so dramatically I can't imagine living the way I did before!! Keep us posted and don't be afraid to ask questions... People here are wonderfully willing to help.

My recommendation... Start at Jacks first blog post and read it and all the comments (serious wisdom there as he answers questions). And keep reading all the blog posts.... May take a who,e but seriously worth your time and you will have many 'aha' moments!!

### David — 2012-05-21T00:58:16+0300

# Thread: Adrenal Fatigue checklist

**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 12

### Steven — 2012-05-15T22:44:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-checklist.1482/post-24352>

Hello everyone,

I want to try Dr. Kruse's protocol to see if it helps my AF.

So this is what I think is needed, please tell me if I'm missing anything.

Paleo diet, cold therapy, lights off after sun goes down, and try to sleep by 10pm...

Anything else that Dr. Kruse recommends?

Thank You!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-16T00:48:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-checklist.1482/post-24406>

Make sure the diet is keto-paleo with lots of fish. Also no exercise but walking is ok in the afternoon (2-5pm-ish) and stare at the sun first thing in the morning for ten minutes to trigger the cortisol.

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-05-16T01:33:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-checklist.1482/post-24419>

I didn't to bed late to start with, so I'm thinking that catching those am rays (coupled with reducing inflammation) is what really helped me. I'm still not on the high end of the range but I figure I'll keep improving.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-16T02:16:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-checklist.1482/post-24434>

Good suggestion about the sun in the morning. I got to be better about that!

> **Patty Cakes;24488 wrote:** Make sure the diet is keto-paleo with lots of fish. Also no exercise but walking is ok in the afternoon (2-5pm-ish) and stare at the sun first thing in the morning for ten minutes to trigger the cortisol.

He also mentions in his blog post foods to avoid and adaptagens that may help. In my experience, the adaptagens made me feel worse - in my lay person observation, they stimulated my adrenals to work and then I had a crash. So, I stay away from them!

The blog post is here

[http://jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/)

highlights:

Pearls for a Adrenal optimization:

Sleep by 10 PM

Sleep in until 8:00 AM

Avoid over training

Do the things you enjoy

Avoid coffee or other caffeinated beverages; steep your tea!

Eat early within thirty minutes of rising

Have a glass of water in the morning with 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of salt

Avoid grains such as bread

Avoid starchy foods such as potato

Mind body mastery/ Laugh several times a day

Take vitamin C, pantothenic acid, magnesium, and vitamin E (Mixed tocophrenols)

Take pregnenolone and DHEA, as needed

Avoid becoming fatigued

Avoid high glycemic fruits

Never skip breakfast ever

Avoid caffeine, chocolate, ephedra, guarana, kola nut, and prescription stimulants. (Ritalin)

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-16T05:40:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-checklist.1482/post-24505>

Unfortunately, I did not do so well with CT, but I have other issues - mostly hormones. I also have Raynaud's and Hashimoto's. SO, I tend to freeze my butt off in normal conditions! I kind of skipped over facedunk and after listening to the It's Rainmaking Time Podcast, I think that was a big mistake on my part. Dr K talks about how facedunks stimulate the vagus nerve and other stuff I can't remember! LOL I did spot CT and then baths. At first it was OK but after three weeks, I hit a wall. Today I see a functional med doc, so if I get my hormones and thyroid straightened out, maybe CT will be better.

However, Dr K was teasing us on the Monster Thread that he has a blog to post about AF and CT with video and testimonial from a client that recovered from near suicide her AF was so bad. So, can't wait to read/see that!

These are the highlights:

Glucose, Serum - 91

Hemoglobin A1c - 5.4

Uric Acid, Serum - 6.1

Ferritin, Serum - 345 (marked HIGH, range should be 13-150)

Cholesterol, Total - 195

Triglycerides - 66

HDL Cholesterol - 71

VLDL Cholesterol calc - 13

LDL Cholesterol calc - 111 - HIGH

T. Chol/HDL Ratio - 2.7

LDL/HDL Ratio - 1.6

C-Reactive Protein, Cardiac - 0.61

TSH - 2.080

Thyroxin (T4) - 9.9

T3 Uptate - 34

Free Thyroxine Index - 3.4

Vitamin D - 75.2

WBC - 8.2

RBC - 5.19 - HIGH

Hemoglobin - 16 - HIGH

Hematocrit - 49.3 - HIGH

MCV - 95

Eos - 15 - HIGH

Eos (Absolute) - 1.2 - HIGH

Testosterone Serum -27

% Free Testosterone - 1

Free Testosterone, Serum - 2.7

Progesterone, Serum -

# Thread: Personal Care products that fit the optimal (non hormonedisrupting chemicals) bill.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 79
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-16T03:55:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-24478>

I have been experimenting with making my own personal care products, and it is so cool. I have given out lots of samples and people flock back wanting more and more. I'm perfecting formulas. Perhaps you will see them at Whole Foods soon!

My "base" for almost everything to go ON the body is this.

Coconut Oil

Macadamia Nut Oil

Cocoa Butter

Then when I want something to smell good, I add my handmade vanilla (made with organic beans and kettle one).

Products so far:

AU NATURAL - just the base, whipped into an easy to spread formula. I use this to remove eye makeup, wash my face in the shower (spread on and wash with just the oil and warm water) and spread on any delicate parts that want moisturizing.

PERFECT PITS - base + baking soda + lots of vanilla. Teh awesomest. Everyone that uses it comes back for more. Can also be used for rashes and itches.

HEAVY WHIP ME CREAM - base + vanilla whipped into a froth. It's edible too! I added to coffee once just to see.

### Glamorama — 2012-05-16T05:51:07+0300

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-16T06:10:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-24514>

Do you have any ideas for natural shampoo to wash my hair with after swimming in the ocean? I do dunk my head under, so the hair gets plenty wet. Normally I wash minimally, every four or five days, but that's impossible with all the ocean swimming I'm doing. There's a lot of stuff in the ocean. Good for me, but not so good for soft, shiny hair.

Any ideas? Conditioner too.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-16T06:21:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-24518>

Other than deo (CO & baking soda, and I do agree that mixing cocoa butter would probably make it easier to use ... it doesn't go bad?) & occasional soap on pits, I don't use anything on my body.

Nothing.

I don't even use makeup remover on the rare occasion I wear makeup. Because I never remove my body's oils intentionally anymore, makeup just comes off by itself after a few hours ... one of the few downsides!

I don't have any problems with the ocean and my hair, which I don't shampoo at all, ever. I do have BIG problems with chlorine. It makes the oils in my hair go nuts, like they go I'M BEING ATTACKED!!!!!!! and overproduce and seize up and it feels like I have a giant blob of wax on my head. I try to rinse in cool water before and after swimming in poisoned pools.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-16T13:14:13+0300

It makes the oils in my hair go nuts, like they go I'M BEING ATTACKED!!!!!!! and overproduce and seize up and it feels like I have a giant blob of wax on my head. I try to rinse in cool water before and after swimming in poisoned pools.

I use barely anything, either. My bottle of Dr Bronner's soap I purchased years ago is still half full and I even use it for other things like washing the floors.

I haven't quite figured out what to do with my hair. No poo doesn't appear to work for me. It starts to get greasy no matter how long I skip the shampoo. I had really short hair for years and it was okay then... just rinsed it every day. Now that my hair has grown to mid-back length I can't rinse everyday - it takes way too long to dry and blow-drying isn't something I want to get into the habit of. My hair actually grew long being no-poo, so it wasn't an issue of not giving it enough time.

I'd really like to skip the shampoo...

### Guest — 2012-05-16T15:44:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-24568>

It makes the oils in my hair go nuts, like they go I'M BEING ATTACKED!!!!!!! and overproduce and seize up and it feels like I have a giant blob of wax on my head. I try to rinse in cool water before and after swimming in poisoned pools.

+1 You rock MamaGrok!! No soap is dope. Friends thought I was weird for not using soap on my face, but I never had pimples or blackheads until I did experiment with washing my face for a time. I am 57 with little to no wrinkling on my face anywhere. Never wore much make-up because it made me look clownish, so nothing of significance to take off.

I also have not used any type of deodorant in over 15 years. A few years back I had a breast exam and the doc said he never felt breast with, absolutely, no lumps at all. I know it's because I have have not suppressed the toxins. I don't have a problem today with stinky underarms.

I try not to miss any of your posts because I know there is always wisdom in them.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-16T16:10:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-24576>

Well, different things for everyone, and I love the idea of mixing a thick butter into the deo I do use (because my body has not yet stopped stinking from the toxins coming out). Everyone has a different situation and should do what's best for that situation and not necessarily what I do! And I LOVE the idea of cavemam's products being in Whole Foods, helping lots of people (b/c face it,not everyone is as crazy as I am) to get rid of the toxins on their bodies. But I do enjoy the ease of a mostly product-free life. It's SOOOOOO easy to camp!!!

I mean, I already typically only shower once/week, and that's water-only. A washcloth and stream plus my little container of CO&BS will take care of my pits. I bring a tiny vial of tooth powder if I'm going to camp a long time, but (confession) I don't brush my teeth every day at home, so it's easy not to while camping. My general rule for the whole body is that if it's not showing or smelling, I don't do anything to it.

Eliminating the shampoo definitely takes some working with it. It took my husband only a few days to get through the over-production transition stage, but it took me nearly six weeks. It was summer, so I just wore my hair up every day. My hair is very different now. The pros are it dries more quickly (used to take 2hrs to air-dry, now only about 45m), has tons of volume (never any thought of needing a styling product), has a natural all-over wave (was always board straight until my third child, when it developed a hideous solitary s wave in just one section in the back!), tangles far less, is fairly water repellent in a light rain, and is super easy. The cons are I can't get it board straight without blow drying now (which I won't do more than once a year because that, too, takes 30m - I have thick hair!), it's not Breck-girl shiny anymore, and requires a short daily preening (much less time than hair care used to take) to keep the oils moving so they don't pile up at the scalp and leave me looking greasy. Another negative is that toxins flowing into or out of my body seem to cause a sebum (grease) response. Another positive is that when I get crap in my hair, like a night at a smoky bar or a wedding with ridiculous quantities of unnecessary stylist-applied hair products ...they're gone by morning. Just like a good sheep, my hair repels stuff.

I recently had to go through the long transition again because I had started using baking soda followed by apple cider vinegar as a wash about once a month. Then I liked the middle ground between Breck sheen & manageability & volume it offered me, and started doing it a little more often and BAM suddenly I needed to do it more than once a week or would be greasy and I was not happy about going there again, so I just cut out the ACV/BS cold turkey and re-transitioned into fully sebum'd hair. I will still use the ACV/BS for special events when I want to do something different with my hair, but not fall into doing it too often again.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-16T18:54:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-24609>

> **Rosekelly;24596 wrote:** Do you have any ideas for natural shampoo to wash my hair with after swimming in the ocean? I do dunk my head under, so the hair gets plenty wet. Normally I wash minimally, every four or five days, but that's impossible with all the ocean swimming I'm doing. There's a lot of stuff in the ocean. Good for me, but not so good for soft, shiny hair.
>
>
>
> Any ideas? Conditioner too.

You might try just putting a little CO in your hair before you go in the ocean as a protectant. The salt is pretty harsh on the hair if you are in the ocean every day. Also you could put your hair up and not get it in the water (Yeah, I know that was obvious). I try to keep my hair out of the water if possible when I go in the ocean.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-16T19:21:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-24619>

@Mamagrok: I was no-poo for about 10 years while having short hair, and it still didn't work for my long hair even though it grew in as no-poo. What do you mean with "preening"?

I would love to be no-poo again.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-16T19:25:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-24621>

> **Suhenna;24650 wrote:** +1 You rock MamaGrok!! No soap is dope. Friends thought I was weird for not using soap on my face, but I never had pimples or blackheads until I did experiment with washing my face for a time. I am 57 with little to no wrinkling on my face anywhere. Never wore much make-up because it made me look clownish, so nothing of significance to take off.

I've pretty much stopped washing my face, too. I stopped using soap/cleansers on it years ago and recently I stopped using anything but a splash of cold water and every once in a while a little face brush. I do like the changes I am seeing... less dry, more even tone, just nicer skin. Never again will warm/hot water touch my face!!

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-16T19:36:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-24623>

> **Souldanzer;24701 wrote:** @Mamagrok: I was no-poo for about 10 years while having short hair, and it still didn't work for my long hair even though it grew in as no-poo. What do you mean with "preening"?

A gentle scalp massage for a minute or two, especially in the most sebum-y areas, then gently moving those oils down the strands toward the ends. This was especially necessary when I was in the transition stage, or any time I ate stuff my body didn't like.

I can't say exactly what will work for everyone, but it was definitely a combination of time and preening and patience. Also, the realization that my hair is nothing like it used to be - better in some ways and more annoying in others. Overall, I believe the benefits (like volume, lack of chemicals, and free time!) outweigh the negatives (like never being a Breck girl (with the completely limp, hang-in-your-face no-volume hair that goes with it) again).

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-16T19:47:45+0300

lol, that would be me on the third day after washing. So then I give in on the fourth day. I'm about to cut it off again b/c I'm tired of dealing with it.

Thanks!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-18T19:33:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-25236>

> **Shijin13;24661 wrote:** ok for the base what's the ratios? are you using straight cocoa butter? (I've got a brick of it TPITA to use b/c its so solid - I can't freaking cut it! I have to shred it to use in cooking) do you heat up all the oils then whip until frothy???? detailed directions pls!!!

Ok, so I still need to experiment and write down quantities. I've just been going by the feel-it method and haven't measured. BUT my best guess is:

1.5 cup of CO

1/8 cup coconut oil (because I get tired of trying to dig it out of the jar it's so hard!)

1/4 cup macadamia

I do melt everything, then babysit it while it cools and whip it every 20 mins or so, so it stays mixed up while cooling.

For the Perfect pits, add just about the same amount of baking soda as you have of goop.

If you have never made Vanilla - MUSTDO. Once you go real, you will never go back.

Order the beans from ebay. it's like $20 for 3/4 a lbd. Get the best rated organic ones. I like both tahitian and madagascar. My unscientific analysis is tahitian is more fragrant and madagascar more taste. . so use one for cooking thing, the other for smelling.

Get a big assed bottle of Kettle One Vodka. Split beans, shove them in vodka. Shake every couple of days for a few weeks, then let sit for a minimum of 6 months while shaking when you think of it.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-18T19:40:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-25238>

I have really REALLY tried to go poo free. Perhaps when I retire. I never could get that fresh clean look - I always looked a little grungy.

I have hope that now I think I'm looking less bald, that mebby I can swing a severe "all pulled back" kind of look with a ponytail. Right now it looks a little too old lady with too much scalp at the part.

No soap on body for over a year. People don't need soap.

So now, I use a little bit of shampoo on my part. Honestly for me, I keep thinking the small dollop that I rinse fairly quickly probably isn't that bad. So I haven't bothered with the baking soda head stuff.

Need to find something tho - any ideas are welcome!

as for no poo - I use wen - I tired no poo - using morrocan method clay "shampoos" & "conditioners" - and my hair went to pieces... ### ealachan — 2012-05-18T20:03:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-25247>

I did conditioner-only washing on my hair (using baking soda and ACV once a week or so, cheapo Suave coconut conditioner every other day to scrub my scalp) for almost a year and it worked well, but then I got a really short hair-cut and when the stylist did it, she not only used a bunch of SLS shampoo before she cut it, but then she loaded it up with all kinds of silicone-based styling gunk when she was done. I was actually pretty pissed about it, and I tried to go back to no-poo (after a good wash to get all the styling gunk out) after that, but the combination of going through the greasy stage again on top of having SUPER-short hair for the first time in like 20 years just sent my self-concious meter into the red and I went back to "normal" shampoo'ing. Still can't quite bring myself to start all over again. Maybe some day when I can take two weeks off so no one has to see me in the grease-ball stage...

The only time I ever use actual soap / bodywash is if I'm REALLY stinky for some reason, which is not often. I do still use traditional deodorant, but I'd like to try the stuff y'all have been talking about and see if it works for me instead!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-18T20:47:41+0300

Ok, sorry for the lack of attention to detail!

1.5 cups of CO

1/8 cup cocoa butter

So, after you melt - and it cools, if it's too liquidy add more CO and Cocoa butter or if it's too stiff (doesn't spread) add more macadamia.

My next batch should be in about 2 weeks - I'll make sure to document amounts then!

Coconut oil and tea tree oil, and gse. For thrush on the bum I use the following: Desitin, lotramin, polysporin, and hydrocortisone - works like a charm - (ped gave us that recipe use 1oz of each - make a big batch and keep on hand!)

### kathylu — 2012-05-21T16:12:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-25930>

I really don't get what's wrong with soap. It's just esterified (saponified) fat. I've been making "superfatted" coconut oil soap for years. The lather is sinfully luxurious and it leaves your skin moisturized, soft and smooth. I think the key is to use as little as possible (I shower every other day) and make sure there are no funky unpronounceable ingredients in it.

I also make my own skin lotion from coconut oil, sweet almond oil, cocoa and shea butter. I usually throw in a bit of vitamin E and C and scent it with organic essential oils...my fave is vanilla oil and orange oil. I smell like a orange creamsicle! You have to keep it in the frig though...no preservatives.

I'm about to try a shampoo recipe...I'll let you know how it goes. I would like to get away from using shampoo (wash my hair every 4th day).

I haven't used deodorant since I was in my 20's. After a few weeks it seemed like I didn't need it anymore. The odor just went away.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-21T17:04:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-25950>

A - most soap isn't soap these days. It's detergent.

B - it's overused. Constantly removing the body's natural oils & flora has consequences, including the inability to make vitamin D from the sun, making girly parts more susceptible to bacterial imbalance (leading to infection), and much more.

I limit soap use to when I'm showing or smelling, and don't hesitate to use it (real soap) when needed.

### johnnyb — 2012-05-21T17:07:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-25952>

I had a case of athletes foot once and 4 weeks of lotrimin didn't do it. I ended up seeing the Dr who proscribed Lamisil which in now OTC. It kicked the crap out of the fungus in a week. I think the information said to use it for one week but expect the rash to take 2 weeks to heal.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-05-22T00:46:29+0300

here are the ingredients:

ACTIVE INGREDIENTS / PURPOSE:

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-31T03:17:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-28467>

Exactly my experience, although I often have to repeat the application a second time if the day is really hot and/or active.

I've also experienced a dramatic decrease in stink, the more I get stuff out of my body that it doesn't care for. It stinks while bad stuff goes in, stinks while it goes out, and stinks far less after it's gone.

Can't remember if I said it on this thread, but I've heard many a tale of farmers working hard in the sun all day with an appropriate (not overly burdensome) amount of sweat, but no stink at all... back in the day.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-31T04:00:03+0300

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-31T17:38:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-28602>

I'm talking \*back in the day\* - literally not reeking. It makes sense to me - I see a considerable change in my personal odor based on whether bad crap is coming in and out of me or not. It makes sense that we wouldn't stink inherently - other animals don't. And I do admit that there is a big difference between the earthy natural smell of a sweating human and the reek of BO. The latter seems common to most everyone today without deodorants & frequent soapy washing. I don't think it was meant to be so.

It makes sense that we wouldn't stink inherently - other animals don't. And I do admit that there is a big difference between the earthy natural smell of a sweating human and the reek of BO. The latter seems common to most everyone today without deodorants & frequent soapy washing. I don't think it was meant to be so.

You know I noticed this on Monday - when it was HOT and HUMID as HELL when we road our bikes upto Arlington National Cemetery w/ the Kids so they could understand why we had the day off.. I was sweating buckets and did not smell at all...

today I'm trying Jack's CO on the pits only to see how that goes... sp far so good - the only problem w/CO is when I have to put on business suits - the CO will ruin them...so I've got to keep up w/the Funk Butter I've been using... I do like to swipe my pits w/a combo of witch hazel, tea tree oil and lavender as another bacteria/stink control!

The latter seems common to most everyone today without deodorants & frequent soapy washing. I don't think it was meant to be so.

well, I was 100 percent raw vegan at the time... unless you'd include that with bad crap (not that I believe it's healthy at this point)... maybe I didn't reek as badly but I certainly needed my shower after work

### Raiken3712 — 2012-07-01T20:40:06+0300

Salt water pools are chlorine pools they use salt to generate chlorine...sadly and from what I've heard the only non-chemical system relies on copper ions and something else called ecosmarte isn't good for a few reasons too. Its supposed to be hard to maintain and the sanitation area isn't very strong either....I'm not sure how much this matters if you don't have many people swimming...if anyone has any experience with Ecosmarte I'd love to hear about it.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-05T16:49:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-35619>

got this one from [http://www.eat-real-food-paleodietitian.com/](http://http://www.eat-real-food-paleodietitian.com/)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/35782=311-deo.jpg)

my natural deodorant experience!

mix baking soda with coconut oil until you get a paste. add essential lavender oil if desired. put in an old deodorant applicator and leave in the fridge (it is in 32C/90F).

apply as needed and smell good without any chemicals/aluminium! it is also a lot cheaper than buying commercial deodorants! PLUS, I love that I could eat what I put on my skin.

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-05T16:51:50+0300

I figure once that's fixed... I shouldn't have it as often... beyond that... I don't have any tips on it... other than eat clean...

I feel so much better not soaking in chlorine!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-35702>

I started w/the Lavender - then moved over to the almond mint... the kids like that one better... I get the 32oz bottle every 4mths or so... I use it to shave - way better than just using the tropical traditions co soap. I'm very sensitive to most cosmetic products - I usually can't use them for more than 1-2mths w/o issues... I've had no issues w/this for my hair or reactions w/my skin - I usually know instantly! I've got such baby fine hair that if I don't use something I end up w/a rats nest - it tangles and gets super knotty. ### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-27T20:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-47511>

> **Lyndra;47958 wrote:** How do you actually get everything to become a nice mixture? I tried making lotion with coconut oil, water, beeswax (following some recipe I found online) and it never emulsified. I ended up with greasy water with a big layer of wax.

I never used water, it didn't work for moi  I haven't used beeswax either, I like CO oil plus a few others. I've used Mac nut and Shea Butter and Coco butter. I like to mix in a little of the Mac as that is liquid at room temp, and as the mix is cooling I keep shaking or mixing it as it cools.

I've not found a way to keep it emulsified and fluffy at warmer temps

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-27T20:42:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/personal-care-products-that-fit-the-optimal-non-hormonedisrupting-chemicals-bill.1490/post-47515>

I just melt everything over a double boiler - stir until its all liquid - transfer to what ever thing I'm storing it in - then pop in the fridge to solidify - w/the shea butter it stays solid at higher temps, I think the combo of more shea and beewax will provide more stability in warmer environments...

# Thread: Low Carb Cruise recovery
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-cruise-recovery.1491/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### David — 2012-05-16T03:58:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-cruise-recovery.1491/post-24480>

First of all, I commend Jimmy Moore for putting this cruise together and everyone involved noted that his staff did a great job throughout the cruise. I do not regret a moment of the time I spent on this cruise, even though my sole reason for attending was to hear **Jack Kruse** speak. I was impressed that the lectures were as high a caliber as I have ever experienced in any venue and the information was certainly well received. Interaction with all lecturers was encouraged and easily done, which was a very great benefit.

However, after a week or so on the Low Carb Cruise, without a bit of cold water for CT (outside of an iced-drink) and somehow gorging on three full meals each day, I managed to gain two pounds or so. I did walk a couple of laps on the half-mile exterior promenade on deck 5 each morning and evening and I was able to spend several hours in the pool one time (all the kids were ashore). The crew wouldnâ€™t allow me to enter the pool before seven AM and the pool water (and the shower, for that matter) was nearly body temperature anyway. I was thankful to have encountered other members of this site, which helped immeasurably in feeling at home.

My pre-cruise stay in Houston was at a classy hotel that had NO ice in the ice machine on my floor, so CT was not possible. However, the first night offered the pre-cruise get-acquainted dinner where I was able to meet Dr. Kruse, so I consider myself to be very fortunate.

By the way, â€œ_Low Carb Cruise_â€￾ refers strictly to the lecture content NOT the menu. I found it fairly challenging to get something close to a Paleo meal, but many others seemed quite satisfied. My GF commented that she was very glad to get home to have â€œreal foodâ€￾ which I took to be a compliment on my cooking.

Two days at home and I have now had several hours of CT, and I seem to be centered once again.

For anyone interested, there were some excellent lectures. I have been informed that all lectures have been videoed and will be available on line. The details are not yet available.

### indigogirl — 2012-05-16T06:15:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-cruise-recovery.1491/post-24517>

Thanks for sharing and welcome back!

### Jillm — 2012-05-20T03:35:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-cruise-recovery.1491/post-25618>

I am trying to talk Jimmy into putting together a cruise out of Brisbane.

### David — 2012-05-21T00:51:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-cruise-recovery.1491/post-25843>

> **Jillm;25701 wrote:** I am trying to talk Jimmy into putting together a cruise out of Brisbane.

Wow! That would be great!

# Thread: A few questions about my test results.

**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Endless — 2012-05-16T09:07:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-my-test-results.1494/post-24544>

I don't think my labs are as complete as some I have seen here...this is what I could get under my medical insurance. I'm listing the ones I am concerned about, not all of them. I'd appreciate any input!

A1C 5.3 (4.0-6.0)

Chol 200

tri 44

hdl 62

ldl 129

chol/hdl ratio 3.21

fasting glucose 5.5 (4.1-5.6)

CRP (low sens) 5.2 (

### Nourish — 2012-05-16T23:48:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-my-test-results.1494/post-24703>

From what I know....A1C & Glucose are creeping up, CRP high, vit D low (want 120-150 in Canadian units), ferritin could be a bit higher, Hormones obviously low & FSH high - are you peri-menopausal? TSH is a little high too. The labs in Canada don't do rT3 anymore from what I've heard. Not sure why.

Same advice as usual....Paleo diet & CT should help sugars

### Endless — 2012-05-18T06:04:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-my-test-results.1494/post-25105>

Thanks...yes, I would say I am peri-menopausal, I'm 43. I am puzzled by the low vit D as I've been supplementing faithfully. I was hoping it would be higher.

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-18T15:19:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-my-test-results.1494/post-25149>

> **Endless;25187 wrote:** Thanks...yes, I would say I am peri-menopausal, I'm 43. I am puzzled by the low vit D as I've been supplementing faithfully. I was hoping it would be higher.

you D3 is good considering the time of year! JAck wants to see his patients at a minimum of 70 heading into to the fall equinox in the N.Hemisphere for winter (Spring Equinox for those in the S. Hemisphere)

mine in Jan was 65mg/dl - I had backed off supplementing for a while (B/c at the end of summer I was at 118mg/dl), then upped it to 5000IU/day from Nov - winter solstice; then cranked it back to 10000IU.... see my pcp next week - going to discuss getting labs run in the next couple of weeks... to see where I'm at...

looks like your lipid panel was a plain jane version - not an NMR or VAP; so your LDL was calculated - looks good, though the longer you're paleo and off grains - the higher your HDL will go which is good!

next time you get labs run - recommend getting DHEA-S as well as Pregnenolone as well..

# Thread: Can I ever enjoy soaking in the hot springs again if I do this?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Endless — 2012-05-16T09:19:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-24546>

I'm sure the answers I am looking for might be somewhere in the blog or the quilt...I have been working my way through all that but have limited computer time. I apologize if this is too basic a question, but I would greatly appreciate either a quick response from someone who has a better grasp on this than I do, or a point in the right direction so I don't have to sift through so many posts! I'm trying to read a little every day but getting anxious to start being proactive and could use a few pointers!

I am on the verge of CT....any day now, I swear! I love reading of people looking forward to it, it gives me hope My question is....is it possible to do CT, become cold adapted and live that way the majority of the time but still enjoy a hot tub, or a sauna, or a hot shower? Will that undo everything, or is it ok to indulge? I enjoy a hot soak in our natural hot springs....do I have to give it up? Or will I just want to give it up? What do you do if you live with someone who is not doing CT....will you be constantly fighting over the house temp?

Silly questions maybe, but I'm curious.....what has your experience been?

### primat — 2012-05-16T09:48:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-24549>

In the Q&A after the last webinar Jack said that while adapting it's not a good idea to heat up by external means - but once you get there it's not a problem.

Edit: also; if you have been properly cold adapted, and then not doing any CT for a few months, it won't take long to get back to the same tolerance.

### neaderthal — 2012-05-16T17:48:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-24600>

> **primat;24631 wrote:** In the Q&A after the last webinar Jack said that while adapting it's not a good idea to heat up by external means - but once you get there it's not a problem.
>
>
>
> Edit: also; if you have been properly cold adapted, and then not doing any CT for a few months, it won't take long to get back to the same tolerance.

Does "external means" include exposure to sunshine or just heated water and artificial heat?

### Ray — 2012-05-16T20:11:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-24638>

> **Endless;24628 wrote:** ....is it possible to do CT, become cold adapted and live that way the majority of the time but still enjoy a hot tub, or a sauna, or a hot shower? ... will I just want to give it up?

I have not done any deep cold therapy soaks; however, with just ~60 degree F soaks, warm water has become significantly less comfortable to be in. It actually stings my skin a bit.

### AKMan — 2012-05-16T20:55:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-24660>

Here's my take on this issue. If you are just getting into CT, you want to teach your body to respond to the cold stimulus. To do this, you employ face dunks, mild showers, mildly cold baths, and work your way over several weeks to really cold baths. During this period, it would be counter-productive to follow the CT session with a hot sauna, hot tub, or hot shower. During this period, you want your body to react to the cold on it's own and warm you from within. This means drip-dry if possible, wear minimal clothing, turn your thermostat down as low as you can.

Once you are used to this, a dip in the hot tub or hot springs would be fine. I find that a hot shower immediately after a really cold bath eliminates shivering completely, is this good or bad? Good if you have an appointment, bad if you want to maximize the cold's effect.

I have a hot tub on my deck. I set it to 90 degrees. It feels really warm, but still refreshing, and since it's lower than my body temp, should be a great happy medium. We have some natural hot springs near our house that are 110 degrees, we go there a couple times a year. Full of awesome minerals!

### janagram — 2012-05-18T17:52:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-25191>

this is a tough one. a real sticking point. I looove my hot baths, hot tubs, etc., but I desire to benefit from CT...so...in our ancestral paleo past I'm pretty sure they never had a hot bath! Yikes!

### chocolate — 2012-05-18T18:16:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-25207>

I started going back in the hot tub while I was on antibiotics and bouncing back and forth to the pool, just to really scorch the sugar. I think I'm soaking up water weight. I weigh more after the pool than before, nekked and dry. I don't worry so much about the chlorine any more, since I learned about those multiple personalities for elements. I drink about a liter and use the restroom a couple times (no idea how much) and weigh about three pounds more. If I had access to local mineral springs, I would think they were circadian. Actually, I do but they seem to be occupied by perves that like to take pix and feel their nudity is glorious. I wish I had the perception altering chemicals they had.

# Thread: Grass fed/wild....how strict, how important?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Endless — 2012-05-16T09:46:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-24548>

I am fairly lucky in my area, I have access (with advance planning) to a side of grass fed beef or a lamb. What about when you eat out, or run out of 'the good stuff' at home? I find myself torn between having enough animal protein and avoding any conventional meat. There is no grass fed beef available in stores here, I have to buy an entire cow or a half, in the fall.

In the meantime, should I supplement my protein with 'grocery store meat', or just do without??

### primat — 2012-05-16T10:08:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-24550>

Add more fish and seafood instead?

### maggie — 2012-05-16T14:10:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-24558>

You can order online, for example from U.S. Wellness Meats to tide you over. They deliver fast.

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-05-16T15:41:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-24565>

> **Maggie;24640 wrote:** You can order online, for example from U.S. Wellness Meats to tide you over. They deliver fast.

unfortunately that can\`t cross the border.

Endless check out Eat Wild BC there are lists of providers for each community and some keep product on hand for smaller orders during the year

[http://www.eatwild.com/products/canada.html#BC](http://http://www.eatwild.com/products/canada.html#BC)

I order from Redl Beef outside of Willie\`s Puddle and you can order small lots. they also show up at many farmer\`s markets but mostly in the Fraser Valley from Lilloet on down so somewhat outside of your area. I got a quarter last year and will get a half this year. in the mean time I am getting some small box amounts of the ground beef which is very good

[http://www.redlbeef.ca/ordering.html](http://http://www.redlbeef.ca/ordering.html)

### nonchalant — 2012-05-16T17:06:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-24593>

I eat a combination of grocery store meat and grass-fed local meats. The grass-fed is gradually increasing. I used to be concerned about getting the better fats from GF meat, but now I'm also concerned about getting the better protein profile from that meat.

But I don't think you should "do without" meat. Conventional meat is far superior to plant proteins.

### ealachan — 2012-05-16T19:39:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-24625>

I eat both conventional and grass-fed, mostly because I can't always afford grass-fed.

### Endless — 2012-05-18T06:06:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-25106>

Thanks everyone, and thanks for the links Colleen! I will check it out.

# Thread: Teeth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth.1499/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-16T16:13:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth.1499/post-24577>

I've gotten really interested in the teeth connection lately.

As a kid and young adult (OMG, how am I not still a "young adult") I had great teeth. Hardly even got plaque. Only one cavity. So much so that I got a little lax with going to the dentist. Last year I decided my teeth felt gross and I needed to go. Well, to my shock and horror - I had 9 cavities! OMG Seriously! I thought they were joking. Now, that I read Dr K's blog [http://jackkruse.com/the-teeth-in-disease/](http://http://jackkruse.com/the-teeth-in-disease/) I am curious. I am highly suspect that something is amuck with my hypothalmus (no TOM and no hormones to speak of) and I have Hashimotos. So, I read the blog but honestly, my brain is not getting it. What the heck does it all mean? I'm such a bottom line person.

So, this peaks my interest:

"He postulated that that dental caries was caused not by bacteria, but caused by inflammation that was due to hypothalamic interruption of hormones that controlled avascular structures in teeth and other endocrine organs"

I've read the advice on supplementing the fermented cod liver oil and K2. But what causes this interruption. And how to you reverse it. I mean, it can't be a co-incidence that I now have 9 cavities and keep getting plaque build up and have no hormones.

Can anyone explain this to me?

THANKS

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-16T16:32:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth.1499/post-24585>

here's a great place to start:

[http://www.sorrentinodental.com/Blog.html?entry=how-to-eliminate-tooth-decay](http://http://www.sorrentinodental.com/Blog.html?entry=how-to-eliminate-tooth-decay)

Jack mentions John in his blog. I actually had a great discussion w/John yesterday on FB. he gave me some great info yesterday.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-16T17:28:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth.1499/post-24597>

Thanks, Shijin.  Great info there but I am really looking for the link to the hypothalmus. How are teeth related to the hypothalmus? How do you turn your hypothalmus on? How does it get turned off? I'm reading google stuff and it says the hypothalmus is the link between the nervous system and the endocrine system. So, that makes some sense for me. My nervous system took a big hit a year and a half ago and now the fallout...I can see why Dr K strongly advocates for making the mind body connection.

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-16T17:43:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth.1499/post-24599>

Check out HPA axis - hypothalmus -Pituary - adrenal relationship - this in-turn impacts the Pituitary - Thyroid axis. I think there can be many causes why the HPA axis will turn off - stress, diet, lack of proper hormone production & leptin sensitivity (or resistance for that matter) - they're all inter-related.

focus on the Leptin Reset - BAB, lunch w/in 4-5hrs after that, dinner 4-5hrs after that (but at least 3hr before bed), get direct sunlight on your eyes 1st thing in the am, and CT. you'll see things reverse. If you have adrenal problems - make sure your supporting w/adaptocrins (maca, rohdilia, l-theanine, Bcomplex and vitamin c), depending on where your diurnal rhythms are - (mine were reversed High at night/low am) so I CT'd during the am. only recently have I been able to CT at night w/o having it impact my sleep - where I'm WIDE AWAKE - CT (even in the form of ice packs around the middle) will impact your adrenals positively

you also need to know where your hormones are at, where your cholesterol is, and what your thyroid is doing. once you have the whole picture - then you can focus on the things you need to work on

as for the carries - how long have you been grain free? I've been gluten free for 6yrs but only grain free in Nov 2010 - Jan 2011 I had my 1st cavity in 37yrs that I had to get filled (6mth earlier I had no cavities) - I was also nursing - I've known several women nursing who've gotten cavities while nursing - not sure why though? diet? I know that the mouth can be the first indication of disease presentation... considering the inflammation I have running around my body its a wonder I only had 1 cavity. this time around no cavities a yr & 1/2 later

# Thread: Fasting + CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-ct.1500/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### youthfuliz — 2012-05-16T16:24:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-ct.1500/post-24583>

Has anyone tried fasting while doing CT? Dr. Kruse? I think I read somewhere AKman fasts once a week? And did a deep CT on a fasting day? Did anyone fast longer than 1-2 days while CT-ing? Just thought that's closer to hibernation... although we have our 2 hours hibernation every night... still fasting for a few days while doing CT may be beneficial?

Of course you need to have some extra body fat before going into a fast so your body has the correct energy source... and you also need to be already a keto adapted efficient fat burner. Anyone thinks this is interesting?

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-05-16T17:49:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-ct.1500/post-24602>

I've been doing it. Mostly because i had been doing IF for several years before know about CT.

I can't say I notice any difference on IF days or not.

### vwaggs — 2012-05-16T17:49:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-ct.1500/post-24603>

I know when I CT if I have an empty stomach, I am shivering like crazy. So if I were to implement fasting, I would do it from dinner to dinner and CT right after my big protein/fat dinner and not eat until the next dinner for a 24 hr fast.

> **vwaggs;24685 wrote:** I know when I CT if I have an empty stomach, I am shivering like crazy. So if I were to implement fasting, I would do it from dinner to dinner and CT right after my big protein/fat dinner and not eat until the next dinner for a 24 hr fast.

I haven't even CT-ed on an empty stomach, let along with fasting. I guess I'll probably try a 24 hour fast first, with CT right after the meal.

### AKMan — 2012-05-16T20:42:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-ct.1500/post-24652>

I have been fasting from dinner Wednesday til lunch on Friday (about 40 hrs) for a few months now. I haven't been doing cold bath CT for a few weeks, but when I was it was fine. No problems from CT+Fasting. In fact, I think the CT helped in that I wasn't hungry and it definitely helped shred some stubborn belly fat.

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-16T22:31:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-ct.1500/post-24677>

I did a 48-hour fast a few days ago and still carried on with my CT in the ocean. No problem. My fast consisted of a cup of juiced kale/cabbage twice a day.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-17T00:05:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-ct.1500/post-24714>
