### PrimalPam — 2012-07-09T02:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-we-fix-how-we-think.1848/post-36266>

My entire family came to spend the 4th with me (I happen to have the perfect view of our downtown fireworks!). Young kids, young adult kids, my late 70's parents, etc.

My sister kept asking me what I eat, because she found literally nothing in my pantry. Of course, I had a lot of food to serve, but they all wanted snacks, prior to the appetizers! I finally started telling them about my Paleo lifestyle, high quality protein & fat, no dairy, no processed foods at all. I told her the pantry is full of stuff I eat...see those jars of CO and CO manna? I eat spoonfuls of it with my meals. She stared at me like I had 3 heads.

But my parents, who know about my past medical issues, actually listened intently when I told them that I switched doctors when the one almost forced me into a hysterectomy due to fibroids and that by changing my diet and supplements (i.e. krill oil) reduced the pain I was having to almost zero, thus negating any need for a pointless surgery.

I recently had bloodwork done, and I put together a 5-year history on a spreadsheet, which I showed my family. Tri's are about half of what they were, HDL's almost double. Sure, LDL's are higher, but my new dr. is going to do a particle density on me, and I'd bet it's a lot of large fluffy.

So, I explained to my sister, I just can't keep that stuff in my house anymore. Thing is, I GET to eat steak, bacon, Ghee, CO Manna, salmon, daily. I do not eat any processed foods, so no, I'm sorry, I just can't keep it in the house anymore, even for visitors. Because I don't even want to support the stupid companies that make the stuff.

### chocolate — 2012-07-09T03:21:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-we-fix-how-we-think.1848/post-36271>

> So, I explained to my sister, I just can't keep that stuff in my house anymore. Thing is, I GET to eat steak, bacon, Ghee, CO Manna, salmon, daily. I do not eat any processed foods, so no, I'm sorry, I just can't keep it in the house anymore, even for visitors. Because I don't even want to support the stupid companies that make the stuff.

I feel the same way. We are in an area of California (high desert, lots of section 8 and dried up golf courses and closed swimming pools) that is more food stamp dense than Mississipi and you wouldn't believe how hard it is to buy groceries. And how infuriating it is to hear folks whine about their diabetes, while the junk food in the carts is obnoxious. The diabetes is huge money in this area and they are opening up several more dialysis centers. Right now our area allegedly needs 70 more techs. A school opened for dialysis techs. The patients can get their nails done and their whiskers waxed (oops plucked... can't wax a diabetic) right after their dialysis. I don't like paying for any of it. It also makes me feel stingy about organ donation.... I want a good story. I don't want to know what someone promises to do tomorrow to be worthy, I want to know what they did.

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-09T03:37:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-we-fix-how-we-think.1848/post-36272>

You know chocolate, I completely agree with you. Except that I look at my parents: mother, T2D and father, quad bypass/multiple stents. The eat "perfectly" according to their doctors. Dad exercises 5x/week with his "cardiac rehab team", who has him on a bike-to-nowhere for 30 mins. They eat low-fat, whole-wheat, no-salt, and I've watched them get progressively worse since their initial diagnoses, approx. 20 years ago.

Mother is a "great patient" and takes her glucophage daily, pricks her finger many times a day, while she makes her fat-free bagel and sugar-free jam. Dad is also a "great patient" and only eats 3-6 oz. of meat weekly, while eating large plates of low-fat pasta with low-fat sauce. Mom is now on Nutri-System, the diabetic plan. She actually called NS to see if all the sugar in the food she was eating was o.k.; they assured her that it was within the ADA guidelines, so she feels great about it! Made me want to scream.

So I see our health care crisis getting worse, but I don't blame the patients. I blame our system, I blame the pharma companies, I blame the corn industry (and the gov't for subsidizing), I blame lazy doctors. Very unfortunately, the Dr. Kruses of the world are rare.

### SeaHorse — 2012-07-09T04:31:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-we-fix-how-we-think.1848/post-36280>

This is a poem by Mary Oliver...I think it fits in this thread:

The Journey

One day you finally knew

what you had to do, and began,

though the voices around you

kept shouting

their bad advice --

though the whole house

began to tremble

and you felt the old tug

at your ankles.

"Mend my life!"

each voice cried.

But you didn't stop.

You knew what you had to do,

though the wind pried

with its stiff fingers

at the very foundations,

though their melancholy

was terrible.

It was already late

enough, and a wild night,

and the road full of fallen

branches and stones.

But little by little,

as you left their voices behind,

the stars began to burn

through the sheets of clouds,

and there was a new voice

which you slowly

recognized as your own,

that kept you company

as you strode deeper and deeper

into the world,

determined to do

the only thing you could do --

determined to save

the only life you could save.”

I think everyone here is on an amazing journey to be celebrated....learning to think well is part of that process...circular thinking, ruminated and obsessing are so self-destructive (I have years of experience). Sometimes a good rant can be cleansing, so have at it, but I want to channel as much energy as possible into being healthy. I hold the space of possibilities for others and do my own work....

This is a great thread - thanks to everyone

### JanSz — 2023-05-05T15:45:46+0300

>

> disease. Most of the time I ignore it but when I'm in bed or standing for a period I can actually feel everything rushing around on the sole of my foot

My feet cramp at night since 1980 or even before that.
I used quinine to deal with that.
With time the problem progressed.
Recently talked with someone and tried the suggestion.
Works miracles, no more nightly cramps.

With both feet, stand with your toes on the step of a stairs.
Lover your heals below the step level as much as possible, then (slowly) squat.
Spend some time hanging, when an inch or two before you reach the end of squatting and you feel the maximum tension on Achilles tendons.
Do that with
straight feet
pigeon feet
duck feet

Better, look at my post here (and the next one):

# Thread: Teenie Leek v2.0
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 96
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-06-13T04:08:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-30799>

Calling this TL v2.0 ... 'cause I used to be on the thin side and my ex husband (Irish/Scot) would call me his teeny little leek, and well I WAS a teeny thing! Now, not so much LOL time has passed, I'm not so teeny anymore, much sicker, and I seek a new, improved version of that teeny girl. I'm a woman now dammit! A goddess!

Been reading daily since March, post rarely, lurk @ the journals of jennifer, ealachan, coldbren, indigogirl, souldanzer, darleen, paulalynn, abetterme, rosekelly. ((hugs)) ladies. I am constantly astounded by how much I have in common with all of you.

chocolate, you are a fabulous asset to this forum. Ditto MamaGrok, ColleenCoble, AKMan, Dali Dula. I;m forgetting others, just trying to type it all out.

Eating disorder thread by tweety....... I could have written that. I have ED background, binge eating/bulimic via exercise. Leptin Reset had me creating all these "rules" WTF where was all the freedom everyone was talking about? I really felt like I was slipping back, esp after the PL meet, described below.

Started Leptin Reset end of Jan, @ 155, slowly lost about three lbs over 2 months, but clearly more body fat than that, I def put on muscle with that. Was visibly leaner. CT cold baths since Feb. Everything was dandy till I had to cut weight for a powerlifting meet in April. Cut from 152 to 148 over course of a week, binged after the meet, gained back the four pounds, plus the 3 I had lost. 155#, puffy and bloated, belly and face, for weeks. Got depressed. All that time, and work, felt like I was grasping at straws. Tried reset again, went UP to 160#. Good grief. During this time I met the fantastical Jennifer and we've been bonding via email and FB and it is helping tremendously. Thanks so much JJ

I can be very frank with people, jennifer can attest to that LOL I need to be frank with myself, (not mean, cause I am already very mean to myself). I need to be frank and look at the way I am sabotaging myself, undermining my efforts.

Journaling, while difficult to maintain, has always been helpful to me.

will start with BAB tomorrow, to quell cravings. Been eating paleo for three years, and while I have lost my asthma and severe allergies, I am still overfat (placement: lean muscular legs, hard round butt, soft loft handles, big poochy belly, thick arms) and still have some lingering health issues. Did 23andme testing, going to meet with new func gyno this Friday, plan to get labs done and book consult with JK when I get results in.

I take a buttload of supplements, read constantly. Quite busy, work 3 jobs, as well as a mother and competitive powerlifter. One positive thing with the weight gain, I have had tremendous workouts. Like ridiculous PRs. 99% of my training is lifting, with the odd conditioning here and there, usually rowing sprints, if at all. When I started my squat was 215 I think, last time I tested early April was 232.5, I won't test again til mid July, I am certain I am @ or near 240 and could be squatting 250 for my big meet in August. And that is raw, no squat suit/gear.

This feels rather disjointed, and almost telegram-ish but whatev. At least I am posting

My new affirmation: I love and approve of myself.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-13T04:12:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-30805>

Become your optimal self........demand it. Think it......become it.

### indigogirl — 2012-06-13T04:29:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-30810>

Hey Teenie!

I used to be teenie, too...(sorry, feeling sorry for myself today).

Looking forward to sharing your journey here.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-06-14T01:32:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-31008>

BAB: 2 burgers, 50g protein total, in coconut oil, with lots of mushrooms.

Was planning on sailing right through lunch like I have in the past, but on a whim I decided to check my blood sugar, I have a glucometer at work. I felt a little "off" but definitely not hungry. 73. It was 1:30, and I knew I wouldn't be eating til 6pm, and would be training at the gym @ 4pm. Felt a little confused... "should I eat? I'm not hungry, but I do feel weird..."

So I ate. For a cafeteria lunch, it was very good, a chicken thigh with roast parsnips and broccolini in butter.

Totally was not hungry, but I immediately felt better. Past few days I have felt awful lifting, a little shaky/wobbly. I haven't really had a problem with hypoglycemia the whole time I've been paleo, just these past few weeks, as my lifting increases, I wonder if I need to up my carbs some, after all it is summer. No fruit, just more veggies, maybe a sweet tater here and there. I bumped up from ~25 to 50g, about 2 weeks ago, felt much better. I hope that isn't "wrong" I really feel so good.

Small dinner, not that hungry, but feel "wasted/empty" again. Small amt of chicken and sausage, 100% dark choc bark.

Not that anyone cares, but I had an awesome training session. Just my and my sweetie in the gym, he was snatching and squatting, I was squatting, pressing and deadlifting. PRs on both squat and press, yet again. 2 days of rest (just walking to stay loose) and back to train on Saturday  I love my barbell.

ALso I was aware that when I was reviewing video ( I tape most of my lifts for my coach, and so I can see what I need to work on) I was just looking at my technique, bar path, speed, stance. Not criticizing my belly or legs or fat arms, whatever negative crap. I was completely objective, as if it were another lifter I was watching. No eye rolling. No sighing. I think I even admired myself at one point, the way my muscles moved under my skin, my hams and quads very defined, my glutes locked, the way my knees slightly wavered at one point and then I quickly drove them out to the sides to steady myself. I could see, I could tell, I LOOK STRONG. wow. I never appreciate myself. This is something new.

I love and approve of myself

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-06-14T02:46:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-31016>

I feel like kicking myself.

An hour after my dinner, cleaning up the kitchen, I demolished a HUGE bowl of the chicken and sausage. WAS NOT EVEN HUNGRY FOR IT. wtf so annoyed at my ...... sabotage? Rebellion? Idiocy? Now my belly is stuffed, i"m bloated, and worst of all, I am down on myself. it was after 7, and I'm to be in bed by 9:30. I'm pissed and frustrated at myself. v1.0 is rearing her head, negative self talk.

ok, at least it was not bad stuff, wasnt cookies or candy. Was protein and fat. And maybe with today's training I needed it (?) but the thing that irks me is that WHILE I WAS EATING IT, I KNEW FULL WELL I WAS NOT HUNGRY. and ate it, ANYWAY. And I feel physically ill now. My potty mouth is going off, I am cursing a blue streak at myself.

15# will not come off like this.

sheesh. ok, dumping everything here, will NOT punish myself for this. Letting it go. Hell, I have to.

I STILL love and approve of myself. I am a good mom, I have good relationships with my parents, in their 80s. I have a nice teeny cottage in the burbs, I have a secure FT job, I have a good relationship with my ex husband, I love my garden, my 2 nutty cats who think they are dogs, and I know I am on the right path. Very grateful for all the things going RIGHT in my life. This is a blip on the radar screen. Gotta stay focused on the right stuff here, the wins, not the shortcomings.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-06-14T20:24:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-31184>

It is possible I am malnourished. I'm working from the assumption I have a leaky gut (is there a way to tell, exactly? I have a high HDL, I know low HDL is a clue) and it is possible I am not eating enough to support my training. IDK anymore. What I don't like about the Leptin Reset is the way I have to NOT listen to what my body is telling me. When I was in outpatient treatment for ED, I had great success with Intuitive Eating. I would eat anywhere from 2-4x day, sometimes only once. I ate only when hungry (and now, I feel I have to IGNORE that, eating first thing in the morning, because I am "supposed to") and I would stop when satisfied, or if unsure, a little beyond that, but not til fullness. Now I feel like I am eating WAY BEYOND SATISFACTION, and into fullness, always with BAB, and with any other meal for that matter.

With Intuitive Eating(IE) we worked on hunger/fullness scales and I always had the option to eat more "later" if I wasn't sure if I was full or not. Now, with LRx, I have to eat and be done with it, there is no "later". I went to a comfortable weight (132#) in treatment that I felt good about it, and it was effortless to maintain. I weighed 132 for years. Here I am 8 years later... 25# heavier, struggling like nobody's business.

This AM: totally NOT hungry for BAB, like really really really NOT hungry. Toyed with following IE, and just eating "later" when I was truly hungry.... but didn't. So full afterwards, it was 2 burgers in CO. 6 hours later, still FULL. Which is fine, to not be hungry now. I rather like skipping lunch, I get my forum fix on my lunch hour

I think life **is** hard, as well as unfair, if you look at things as black and white. Depending on how I were to look at things, my life is either _super awesome (I am a competitive athlete! I am following my dreams! I went backpacking through Peru, hiked the Inca Trail! I've lived in 3 different countries! I have a fab teen age daughter! Rented an apt in Paris with her for 2 weeks! I can squat 1.5 times my bodyweight! I can speak Spanish and French! Woo Hoo!)

_

(With all my training and strict keto-paleo, I am still overfat, boo hoo. Why don't any of my siblings have the health problems I do, boo hoo. Why didn't my marriage work, boo hoo. Why do I have to work 2-3 jobs, why can't I make more money, boo hoo. Why did I have 2 miscarriages, boo hoo. Why don't I have a bigger bank account, woe is me, wah wah wah!)

Don't get me wrong, I still have days where I wonder why things are the way they are, but I generally look at life with the positive view, not the negative/unfair/hard view.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-16T23:37:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-31624>

When I had my lumpectomy, I was informed that it is very common for women to find lumps on their own. I found mine. I think that knowing/getting to know your body in that way really is the best prevention.

Despite having been faced with the possibility of breast cancer, I still stick to no mammograms. I never allowed my doctors to do a mammogram for diagnostic purposes when we were trying to figure out if my lump was benign or not. The last thing I wanted to do was to shoot x-rays into already suspect tissue. That took a lot of willpower b/c the first thing every single nurse asked me to do was a mammogram. I insisted, and eventually ultrasound worked just fine.

Never will I get a mammogram. Squeezing the tissue like that might as well injure any present malignant areas and spread the cancer cells into your bloodstream. I got a lot of info from a scientist friend who works in breast cancer research right after finding my lump and one of the things she said was to leave it alone as much as you can b/c injuring the tumor or tissue around it has the potential to either spread it or "wake up" non-active cancer cells.

### indigogirl — 2012-06-16T23:41:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-31625>

So ultrasounds are OK? They have been doing both on me (mammogram and ultrasound b/c I'm fibrocystic with dense breast tissue).

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-16T23:45:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-31626>

I have no idea and am guessing is depends on your condition. I was pretty sure that what I had was a cyst and cysts show up on ultrasounds, so that is what we chose to do. My lump couldn't be found with ultrasound, iodine treatment wasn't working, so eventually I decided to stop the madness and have surgery.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-06-17T18:16:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-31757>

Past 3-4 days been a whirlwind. Food/meals are an afterthought, which is good.

BAB on both days, felt very "nourished" from my food. Nice feeling deep inside.

Emo update: boy have I created a \*\*\*\*ton of drama in my life. Looking back on this week, I see MAJOR errors in thinking and behavior where I clearly do things to create havoc so I do not have to \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*. Oh boy. Started with my bare all confessions to Jennifer, (thank you so much!) I've been able to say, and release, things that I have been carrying around with me. For years. Awful guilt and shame, gone. Or maybe not GONE, but not the anchors that they were. GOd, did I give those feelings POWER.

By letting that go, I swear that I can actually FEEL the giant gaping holes in my heart and how I am frantically thinking of how I can fill them back up with MORE drama. Oh my god, this has to stop, I am stopping it. I can see and feel it so clearly now. Why now, I am not sure but who cares, I just know so clearly what I am running from and why I keep effing my life up. Why I keep having the same drowning nightmares, why I can't lose weight, why I am unhappy. Ugh. While it was initially depressing, I feel very strong with this knowledge. What can I \*do\* with this knowledge... I don't know. I can't just get up and walk away.

I have such an impulse to RUN AWAY to the mountains. not forever, just a few weeks, be alone with my thoughts, and come home with the answer(s). doggone it, I need a sabbatical, a retreat! Sheesh.

Food is now an afterthought because I am thinking and dealing with REAL issues, instead of making food and weight my impostor issues.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-06-21T02:57:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-32569>

HAPPY SOLSTICE ALL

Very hot here on Cloud 9 in the Dirty Jersey. 99F. I don't have central air in my house, just a lil bedroom unit. Just put it in today. Whew!

HAB this am, was getting warm even @ 7am, couldn't force myself to have the whole thing. Only half, I brought it to work, had the other half @ 1pm, even though I wasn't that hungry for it. I knew i was lifting @ 4pm, wouldn't be eating til 6pm.

Trained today in the furnace. Gym was a balmy 85, I kept the windows and doors closed, much cooler than outside. Had a nice mental breakthrough with training. Squatted 210 for 4, was supposed to do 5. Felt so heavy and the heat had me sapped, so I racked it after the 4th rep. Walked away, toward my water bottle. Stopped. Went right back, unracked it, did 2 more reps. 210 has been such a mind-eff for me. Would ditch/bail/dump, such a struggle! I will still repeat this next Wed, cause technically I didn't do all 5 one after the other and I don't even have to ask my coach about that, he will say "yes. Do eet over. and faster" I should be steadily repping 220 like this in August, which really puts 240 as a very realistic goal. KEWL! And to continue in this strange language regarding my odd yet ridiculously satisfying passionate relationship with my barbell: I power cleaned 115 from a high hang position. Purty happy with that too. DL was ummm, not so awesome, failed @ 275 but whatev, 2 outta 3 aint bad.

So I come home a sweaty mess to an even HOTTER HOUSE because my 15 yo DD BAKED CHOCOLATE CHIP COOKIES.

Jesus Mary and Joseph child! 1) WHY on the hottest day of the summer 2) I'm starving, thankyouverymuch 3) kitchen is a bloody mess, flour, baking sheets, measuring cups all over... how do I make dinner now?!?!

REASON: it was the last day of school, duh, Mom.

Freaking out ON THE INSIDE. Ate big chunk of 100% choc. 2 brazil nuts. Cookies smelled soooooooo good. Told her pack em right now, and that stuff has to go to her dad's. Not here.

I ended up eating a pound of scallops LOL wtf I didn't mean to, I don't even know if I was that hungry for them, but I felt so stressed about the cookie invasion and the mess and the heat onslaught. lately all I want are scallops and mushrooms. or burgers and mushrooms. or salmon and cilantro ( I use inger's recipe with white wine vinegar)

But a POUND? I think ealachan ate a pound of shrimp once. And inger too, a pound of fish in one sitting. Sounds crazy! But that makes me feel better.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-07-06T01:00:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-35728>

wow has it really been that long since I was here? Sheesh.

well, the weight loss musta been a fluke. Came and went. I was pretty upset about it. I have to stop the relationship with the scale. Just no good comes from it. It is ...frightening... to think of that. Like I am giving up, throwing in the towel, letting myself go. But it only makes me miserable.

Mental stuff has been getting worse. Pretty sure I absolutely hate myself. Which is upsetting to realize. At the root of all my problems. I think though, that it is a case of forgetting that I hate myself, and then I cope well and adjust to things, and care for myself. Then I remember what I have done and who I am and that I am not worthy, and I start to withdraw, ED behaviors surface, and the depression falls on me like a ton of bricks. Does that evenmake sense LOL that when I forget to be miserable and unhappy with myself, I actually do quite well. It is very hard for me to be happy. Even though I like it, the way I feel when I am happy, I feel like I don't deserve and should cut it out. Guilty.

Just kinda rambling, need to vent/journal.

i just re-read this. Imagined my 15 yo DD had written this and started to cry. I need to get past this \*\*\*\*. i'm 40 effing years old. 30+ years is too long to keep this guilt and shame. Doesn't serve me at all. but.. I almost feel like I don't know how else to \*be\*. I have been this way for so long. But I don't like it anymore. I am always running, chasing things that I think will MAKE me happy, instead of just BEING happy.

i guess i think i can't BE happy, because there is nothing about me to be happy about. Jeez I sound like an angst ridden teenager LOL at least I can still laugh.

Jen, you have been such a tremendous help to me the past few weeks! Today was full of gems from you.

While my powerlifting has been going ridiculously well, I decided this morning to **not compete in August. The relief I have felt all day from that decision confirms it is the right thing for me. Not the competition, but the making weight class was really stressing me out. AND YES I KNOW i could compete in a higher weight class! but not ready for that**. So, it is better for me to pass this one up. I squatted 215 for a triple yesterday. Super duper stoked 'bout that. And then pause benched 125 for a triple. Deadlifted 245 for a double. and then hold onto your hats, I did 27 dead hang pull ups. No, not all in a row  in sets of 4s and 3s. Resting today and tomorrow.

I feel better after venting, thanks.

### chocolate — 2012-07-06T01:33:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-35730>

My favorite joke about fitness is by Lewis Black.... paraphrasing... He goes to the International House of Pancakes. He gets a pitcher of coffee, sits around and drinks it. He looks around and everyone is fatter than him. He finds his happiness in IHOP. I love that you find your happiness in strength. Just being strong would make me happy. I have been weak a few times and its mind boggling to me. I also had someone stronger than me threaten to kick my a\*\*. Not a good feeling. She was sick. She could have taken me and I am very scrappy. What a bod she had and ten years on me. She was a stray human, for lack of a better word. The cops kept picking her up , feeding her in the jail over night, and sending her to anger management. She lived in the park and was tougher than me. It really is all about perspective. I could have only been as tough as her if I lived like her and her physique was stunning.... kind of like Michaelangelo's David. She has nothing and gets what we all want for free. Ironic how things are, eh? We won't talk about the ample bosomed Joan of Arc that rides her horse through the park. Gravity found her and none of your bras would fit her.

I hope you feel better and I'm glad you are relieved about cutting down on the over scheduling. The skies are much bluer when you eat grease and all of you socks are mated.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-06T02:41:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-35744>

the thing about forgiveness is that you won't give it until you accept the present facts as they are...people don't forgive because they think that somehow holding on gives them some chance at changing the past. When in fact, the only way to change your present and affect your future is to forgive. Of course, if you throw the whole time is relative phenom in there, I guess forgiving now does change the past. Accept the NOW and free yourself from the past. You don't hate yourself. You just want a different past.

If you only knew how energetically freeing loving yourself as is would be for you and for your daughter, you would do it in a nanosecond. Moms are like that. Change your dna and you change your daughters.

### kcorcoran@comcast.net — 2012-07-06T02:45:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-35745>

@ Jennifer, wow beautiful what you said for teenie... and all us gals who have issues with ourselves.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-07-06T03:06:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-35756>

chocolate, I love about being strong...I guess it relates to my childhood, where all my issues stem from being weak and being physically overpowered. And then, emotionally unable to \*be strong\* and stand up for myself. To know I had done nothing wrong. Was not guilty.

Now I am so strong I could kick anyone's ass. Without even trying. Never made the connection before.

Not that anyone could ever hurt me as much as I hurt myself.

I'm strong enough to see all this now. I'm strong enough to stop the bull\*\*\*\*. just stop it already.

### chocolate — 2012-07-06T03:12:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-35762>

You gotta live to love... that's all I know for sure.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-07-06T03:19:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-35769>

JEN. wow. i think i know WHY i haven't been able to forgive myself all these years. jesus mary and joseph because i am trying to forgive myself for the wrong thing. no wonder it hasn't been working. DERP \*facepalm\* I can't forgive something that wasn't my fault in the first place. And beating myself up because I handled it poorly, ummm that's not working too well. Correction, I didn't handle it poorly, I handled it as a child would. Because I **was** a child. And beating myself up because I "allowed" it to happen, no longer valid. I see the lie that I created in my head. And then I believed that lie. Til now.

I never got angry. Never felt outrage. Never felt hurt. I felt guilt and shame. Sunnavabeech, I'm sure feeling it now.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-07-11T01:55:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-36680>

Been several days since I broke up with Ted. My scale. Who feeds ED, so I had to dump him. Poor guy. He'll get over it

Not having a number to guide how I feel about myself is weird. I've done this before, so I just have to get used to it. The NOT weighing that is.

In other emo news, since I have not been foolin' myself with the smokescreen of weight obsession, I am now working on the pickle of twisted relationships that I am in... oh boy. Very hard. I see that I am more stable now than I was since last few posts, thank god, I was going a lil off the deep end there, kind of hysterical, not in the funny ha ha way either.

I need to have a very direct conversation with ===, I need the answer to something and I am scared of the answer but I feel I need to know. What good will it serve me? I'm not sure. There are only 2 responses she can possibly give, and if she gives me the "wrong" one (the one I suspect) I think it is part of the healing process, trying to let go of what happened. I think. Or maybe I am just prolonging the pain by digging deeper into it? I have never been able to talk about this without crying, hence, I never talk about it. Eyes are watering as I type this, just thinking of it all. Some people here have spoken about letting things go, and moving on. I think I just chose the "forget it even happened" route and that didn't work out too well (well I still have huge gaps in my memory, but thats ok LOL I dont wannna remember it all LOL) I have been crying a lot about it lately, just feeling the loneliness pain and even the shock of it too, and it is helping. I have been thinking of the little me, and instead of being frustrated with her, I feel such overwhelming compassion now. My throat hurts. I want to scoop me up and rock me in the old rocking chair, say "there there, I'm here now, I gotcha baby..."

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-07-11T02:06:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-36682>

in PHYSICAL news, I feel so much better. SLEEP, where have you been all my life?!?!

Past few days been getting anywhere from 7-10 hrs. ooooo emmmmmmm geeeeee I feel groggy and sluggish in the morning but not in a "I need to go back for 3 more hours" kinda way. Its more like I woke up from the dead. Really. Ever wake up from an unexpected nap when you were sick and slept like 3 or 4 hours, and you wake up and are just .... blinking. For a long time? That is how I feel, like I'm coming out of anesthesia. WTF, but I LIKE IT

Im back on eggs and tomatoes, the AI was proving to difficult to maintain with above emo stress, and I figured lemme loosen some constraints before I explode from the pressure. Smart move. I can always take them out again. Right now, eggs help.

Lifting tomorrow, very psyched to squat and press. been seeing very good gains with these two lifts. Rode my bike to the gym yesterday, will go for a short ride to the reservoir tonight, I love watching the sunset over the water. Then its blublockers, cuddle time with the fam and lights out.

called func med doc, could not move my appt up from July31, argh. When I get my results at the appt, will book consult with JK. Will be my birthday present to myself, Aug 2 I will be 41  I love me

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-07-13T02:41:31+0300

well I guess it had to happen...I am not immortal LOL I had a bad day in the gym. The iron won.

I was kinda distressed over 2 patients. Sometimes I feel like I accomplish nothing at the hospital. But every now and then, there is a little flicker.

Back at the iron on Saturday. I will win.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-07-13T03:17:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-37374>

good emo news, makes me see I worry about my baby girl TOO much.

She is really thriving in summer culinary school (for HS students) Totally in her element. Cannot shut her up  She talked non stop for 45 minutes when I got home, telling me all about today. And of course I was beaming, so happy. My sweet baby has been through way more than most people, such trauma, and to see her blossoming, my heart is really so light. She has a great new therapist, they are working through stuff left and right. This kid is brave. The baggage I carry? She will have nothing like it. She is an old soul, this one. I've told her she has healed many generations of their hurt. We fire-walked last year when she was 14, an initiation. I think we need a sweat lodge this year.

There is nothing sweeter than this girl. My heart is outside of my body.

### jihad4o — 2012-07-13T09:27:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-37441>

> **Teenie Leek;37517 wrote:** Sometimes I feel like I accomplish nothing at the hospital. But every now and then, there is a little flicker.

Most people at the hospital are sick and when I say "sick", I mean "emotionally attached to sickness". [http://www.nlm.nih.gov/medlineplus/news/fullstory\_126845.html](http://http://www.nlm.nih.gov/medlineplus/news/fullstory_126845.html)

They just can't let go. They are angry about it. They are sad about it. You can give the best medicine to their body(meaning exercise, food, air and sun) and they still can't be "treated". They just can't let go.

Often times I meet people and they are old...like in their soul. Their bodies are already coffins that drift trough life. And then you meet someone that has the spark of life in them and that gives you hope...

sorry to vent it out here, but I just really connected with that feeling.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-07-13T15:49:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-37468>

> **jihad4o;37608 wrote:** sorry to vent it out here, but I just really connected with that feeling.

oh vent away, makes me feel like I'm not the only one...Thank you.

So my sleeping continues to go great guns.... holy tap dancing Moses, I cannot believe how amazing I feel. This morning I slept thru SO's alarms, only waking when he got out of bed, I was super groggy. Sat straight up, removed sleep mask, and just blinked. For a long while. I took a cold shower, breakfast was 2 burgers(50g pro) with shrooms, could barely eat half. I feel so rested. Calm.

I have been seriously sleep deprived, methinks. I think my mood issues are coming from this.

Crazy dreams continue: guess I will start writing them down. One had a co-worker was suggesting nude sunbathing in OBX (where we have both taken our families on vacation) In the other, my lil sis had to shave her head b/c she was developing some kind of alopecia. Fairly normal I guess, I can sometimes have dream that are just humdingers. my fave, lucid dreams. Those are awesome. Grateful I can do that.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-08-07T20:24:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-43471>

Has it really been that long since I posted here?! Wow. I read every day, nearly every single thread. Feel like I really know many of the folks out there. Crazy.

Anywayyyyyys, sleep is still going GREAT. I am sleeping minimum of 7 hrs straight and still quite often 9. It feels so good. I no longer have dark purple circles under my eyes.

Started BHRT last week, 10:1 progesterone/testosterone I take 1ml cream in the syringe at night. also func med doc started me on 3mg sublingual melatonin. Too soon to tell when I would see results from the BHRT. Anyone?

I will attempt to post my labs later, honestly there were 24 pages that I scanned to JK, I cant imagine typing all that \*\*\*\* out. Which reminds me, consult with JK is Saturday. very excited.

Eating: has become a Thing I Do. Like Brushing My Teeth. No head games. Sometimes I'm bored with it. 50% from the sea, raw and cooked, rest is beef, tiny bit pork. Veggies: meh. Fruit: meh. Sauerkraut, yezzir.

Working out: hard to say if I went into overtraining? Dunno. Took week and half off from lifting, went back to squatting light (for me) and yesterday topped out at 185 x 5. Happy with that, though I did PR in mid July with 207 x 5. But whatev. My pullups are still good. I think I would cry if I couldn't do pull ups anymore

I am definitely leaner. No doubt. No idea what I weigh, but it is so noticeable in my face, arms and belly. Might only be 4-5 lbs, but I see definition in weird spots, like the upper pecs, forearms. hmmmmph.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-08-07T20:26:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-43472>

OMG teh biggest thing from my labs.......after FOUR FRIGGIN' YEARS of positive ANA with up and down titers..............

NEGATIVE ANA

wut. I didn't even think that was possible.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-08-07T20:34:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-43476>

just changed my siggy

1) no longer have the POSITIVE ana/?lupus? dealio

2) just had a birthday!

I am so loving myself right now. Started back up with my yoga. Been hiking and kayaking again. Had the consult with func med doc who started me with the BHRT. Made the appt with JK. Thinking about taking a SOLO vacation to see my sister who lives in Japan. Good things are happening all around me, but most importantly, in my head, I feel good. no more hateful thoughts.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-08T01:02:18+0300

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-08-14T04:26:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-44840>

Well for a chickie so low in testosterone and GH, I must be succeeding in the gym by sheer will power

Squatted triples, not PRs, but good considering I had a few weeks layoff. 185-190-195. 3RM PR is 215. I pressed 80 for triples too, the press is the bane of my barbell existence. I'm a good deadlifter, a great squatter and a terrrrible presser. oy. magically I pressed 90 for a single, that was a happy discovery. Not a PR either, it's my current 1RM but shoot, that's good for me.

Then pull ups. Think I have to start doing these weighted again, they are not really progressing as I'd like. Then again... in light of my terrible hormone panel, I guess I should be happy I can even do them! This could really explain why I've struggled with my PL over the past year, linear progression has been difficult, yet not impossible, but still very very hard. My coaches were totally stumped as to why I'd keep stalling, and I was getting very frustrated with myself, thinking I had perhaps just "topped out" and could not get any stronger.

When I get my hormones in order, who knows what I will be lifting. With crappy hormones I squatted 232.5, with OPTIMAL hormones......!?!? eeeeeeeep, exciting just to think about

So pull ups: gonna put on a dip belt, start with 5# and go from there.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-08-25T21:09:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-47090>

Here I is!

Super duper busy, which is good and bad.

Good because my small business is g-r-o-w-i-n-g by leaps and bounds. I know I am blessed to have my hobby/passion/avocation evolve into my vocation. I still can't leave my FT work at the hospital, but at least now I can see that YES it will be a reality

Bad because the stress is wack. My sleep which HAD been awesome, is terrible again. To stop the onslaught, I started delegating a LOT more things to others, and the rest of the \*\*\*\* that I can't do, doesn't get done. BFD. I have really lowered my standards on stuff, and just have to accept that I will have a dirty house and crappy yard for a while. Oh well. I've taken to giving away a ton of "stuff" lately cause I just don't want to clean around/take care of any more \*\*\*\*. I have too much stuff.

FINALLY got my GI effects kit in the mail. Oh goody this looks simple and fun. Not LOL

Awaiting salivary cortisol...pretty sure its not good.

My dad has vascular dementia. Not good. Makes me sad. Been spending a lot of time with him and my mom lately (also why I'm stressed and busy) Always give them huge amounts of seafood, which they appreciate and just gobble down. Today gonna spend a lot of time with him, want to go on a walk with him, I bought him a neat (I think) walking stick/staff. He refuses to use a cane, but the walking stick...he looks a bit like a Gandalf, well, bald and no beard  makes both of us laugh.

So when I'm with him I am happy, and stay in the present moment, enjoying him and our time together. When I leave, that s when the tears are unstoppable, rolling down my face and my throat hurts. One unusual thing: he was always very detached emotionally when I was growing up, never huggy or expressing feelings. Now, every phone call, every meeting ends with him saying " I love you" and a pat-on-the-back-hug. That's so nice.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-08-25T21:32:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-47096>

I always like to end on a high note in the gym, so lemme do that here in my journal, last entry was kinda sad.

So back 2 weeks ago...

I squatted 185 for triples and pressed 80 for doubles.

Yesterday squatted 205 for triples and 85 for doubles.

And with ZERO pull up practice this month just banged out 5 in a row, full extension at bottom, throat to bar at the top. KEWL.

Just been doing my powerlifting, no conditioning, but much less intensity than I had been doing all spring and summer. Was doing Texas Method, if anyone cares. Guess I'm doing a modified TM now, alternating press with push press, no benching, and just going back and forth between a heavy and a light squat. One day I got wacky and just power cleaned. Worked up to 115, pounds not kilos haha (that will only be funny to like 1 other person) baby weight, but looking at it with the new perspective of my hormonal profile... pretty damn good.

I'm almost always happy in the gym. Pleased with my progress, excited about where I'm headed.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-08-25T21:40:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-47098>

Awww. That is nice. I always feel all the repressed stuff comes out when ppl get dementia or Alzheimer's. It was always there! Xoxo

He refuses to use a cane, but the walking stick...he looks a bit like a Gandalf, well, bald and no beard  makes both of us laugh.

Tons of coconut oil/MCT oil for your daddy me thinks.

I saw this young woman at the hospital the other day.... hooked up to portable oxygen and needed a cane to walk. She had it all figured out. The tube of the nasal canula was attached so it went down on the back of her head under her shirt so all you could see was the little tube that goes in your nose, no tubing hanging down the neck and chest. And her cane was matched in design and color to her outfit. I bet she has a huge collection at home and has come to see it as a fashion accessory. I just thought she was really cool. As I still rip the nasal canula off my face when it knocks on the door.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-09-13T04:03:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-51348>

06:00 - 08:00 AM 7 **Depressed 13-24 nM

11:00 - 1:00 PM 7 Normal 5-10 nM

04:00 - 05:00 PM 3 Normal 3-8 nM

10:00 - Midnight 2 Normal 1-4 nM

Cortisol Load: **19 23 - 42 nM

The cortisol load reflects the area under the cortisol curve. This is an

indicator of overall cortisol exposure, where high values favor a catabolic

state, and low values are sign of adrenal deterioration

Depressed morning cortisol, < 13 nM, is suggestive of marginal

HPA (Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Adrenal) performance. Normal rhythms

exhibit highest cortisol value for the day at 7 - 8 AM.

The Cortisol release inducers fall into 4 broad categories shown in the

adjacent flowchart. Long term adrenal axis maintenance and restoration,

require optimization of all the cortisol inducers.

Dehydroepiandrosterone Free \[DHEA + DHEA-S\]

Pooled Value >25 Elevated** Adults (M/F): 3-10 ng/ml

Insulin

Fasting 130 pg/ml

Total Salivary SIgA 15 U/ml

Got this in the mail today, will post separately as well. My cortisol graph wasn't exactly flatlined, but goddamn, close enough. No wonder I am so friggin' beat. I feel like I could go to sleep for the night at 3 pm. Sigh.... I read in one of the many blogs about light first, then something else, then hormones. Light could be better I guess. I'm already taken adrenal support caps, guess I need more/different ones.

My dad is getting worse. I love him so so so much. The worst part about all this is knowing that he is acutely aware that he is losing himself, and dependent on us. He is not angry about the dementia, as I have seen in other patients. He knows he needs help and asks for it. In July, he was searching for some word, and I said, " Daddy, (yes I'm 41 and call him Daddy, wanna make sumptin of it?) do you want to keep trying for the word, or do you want us to help you figure it out?" and his voice cracked a little, and he said "no, I know I can't figure it out. Please help me." Broke my heart. I can hear him saying in my head.

GOOD NEWS: my mama 81, is wearing blue blockers, doing mild CT (face dunks), grounding @ the beach, sun gazing @ sunset, and eating gluten free. Awesome. She even said she will go in the ocean with me this fall WOOT. She is a tiny little bird of a woman who has outlived almost every one of her ancestors by THIRTY YEARS**. She is really embracing JK, she loves to listen to the webinars  I am a starfish, hurray! And so is she, she convinced her best friend to get blue blockers!

Soul: She has had my dad on coconut oil for some time, and everytime I visit, I douse them in seafood. Dad is harder to get to eat gluten free, but we do our best.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-09-14T07:40:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-51612>

> **Teenie Leek;51933 wrote: In July, he was searching for some word, and I said, " Daddy, (yes I'm 41 and call him Daddy, wanna make sumptin of it?) do you want to keep trying for the word, or do you want us to help you figure it out?" and his voice cracked a little, and he said "no, I know I can't figure it out. Please help me." Broke my heart. I can hear him saying in my head.

> GOOD NEWS: my mama 81, is wearing blue blockers, doing mild CT (face dunks), grounding @ the beach, sun gazing @ sunset, and eating gluten free. Awesome. She even said she will go in the ocean with me this fall WOOT. She is a tiny little bird of a woman who has outlived almost every one of her ancestors by THIRTY YEARS**. She is really embracing JK, she loves to listen to the webinars  I am a starfish, hurray! And so is she, she convinced her best friend to get blue blockers!

I love the honesty in which you embrace your father's condition. I used to work on a metastatic cancer ward. The patients all knew they were dying and most wanted it openly dealt with. It was the family who pretended like things were OK... because they couldn't bear the pain of the other. Such a small interaction such as asking your father if he wants help with a word has such depth and caring to me. You meet him honestly on the road he travels. No matter how painful. And in doing so, you say that you, too, can bear the pain. And that you walk alongside him.

And your mum, well just amazing! Such a wonderful wonderful story. You are a starfish, for sure. Love it.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-09-15T02:47:21+0300

geeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee

I am so happy, I am doing the happy dance, just stupid happy

I can compete! I thought with my DHEA and BHRT (taking T with Pe) that I'd not be able (they are classified by WADA as PEDs)

Basically have been half assed training since late July when I started the new regimen, figured I needed to choose health over the meets. I assumed I couldn't compete, so I didn't even ask. Silly goose.

re-reading WADA website last week, they have a therapeutic use exemption........ long story short, got word today I am good to go.

I qualified for Championships back in April, meet is in Nov.

My plan is to resume my training with same half assed intensity because I have STILL been making gains in the gym, so why do anything MORE, and cutting out the 1 day of conditioning I used to do when training for a meet (to help make weight), and just stay completely in powerlifting phase. I know JK doesn't believe me, he thinks I am a conditioning junkie, but whatev. I know how I train is the right way. THE TIME that I train.... however has to change.

BS 4x5@190 so a volume day on squat. have done 5x5 in the past, training formally for comps. Over the summer found 3x5 produced \*prett good stimulus, but not quite enough. Not worrying about weight, just yet. I am 6# over my class.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-09-19T04:19:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenie-leek-v2-0.1849/post-52600>

found this good explanation of fat malabsorption and fat maldigestion

Malabsorption occurs when the gastrointestinal (GI) tract cannot take up a dietary compound. This is caused by the loss of function of the cells responsible for absorption.

Maldigestion occurs when an important digestive enzyme or tissue is lacking or not functioning correctly. This may be caused by genetic disorders, injury to the tissue that provides the enzyme (i.e. the pancreas), alterations in pH that make the enzymes inactive, or to surgery.

In general, clinicians speak of both disorders as malabsorption disorders since they both result in a lack of absorption of nutrients.

hmmph.

This morning in the gym, light day for squat, volume for bench.

BS 2 x 5 @ 160

BP 4 x 5 @ 102.5 kinda lame, oh well. I'm a much better squatter

switching training to morning is already helping. With afternoon training, even with no conditioning, I would perk up and get kinda wound up I suppose. and my morning wake up alternated from 5:20 when I had to work at the gym, to 7:00 when I didn't. So now I always get up at 5:20, and go to the gym, either to train or work. I feel better having the set wake up time.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-14T05:07:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-labs.1851/post-31067>

Looks like a classic pregnenolone steal.......Your SHBG is a bit up so that could be due to many factors but because your Lipid panel is what it is I think your free T3 is really a big problem for LDL conversion. You are particle A. It appears with your T level your DHT is decent but I am assuming you have to be on arimidex because you should be aromatizing the excess and youre not. Andro level is also quite low. I think for sure you have a serious cytokine problem gunking up your enzymatic pathways.......you might consider a cytokine array panel and a HS CRP. .......E2 is up but with your T level not surprised. B12 level is low for the amount of T and estrogens you have.......with that high level of T you must be aware of your E2 at all times with your SHBG. Your DHEA is really low on one panel and better on the last one......Your Rev T3 is terrible and this backs up my belief that you have some serious inflammation somewhere and your leptin resistant likely at the liver and gut level. My bet is you also have a leaky gut.......real leaky too and it you likely have adrenal fatigue.......and that tells me TNF alpha and IL-6 are part of your problem. Im also a bit shocked your HCT is not higher given your T levels.

# Thread: Gettin my head in the game
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 39
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-06-13T21:27:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-30953>

Alright, I'm going to start a journal and maybe that will help get my butt on track. I've been following a low carb diet on and off for many years. I've struggled with hypothyroidism for about 10 years, but I seem to be pretty well regulated on armour thyroid right now. I've struggled with being slightly overweight my whole adult life, but since having kids it's gotten worse. I gain weight like crazy while I nurse my babies. I don't know what's wrong with my body that it likes to pack the pounds on while I nurse. I currently weigh as much right now, when my babies just turned 1, as I did last year right before I delivered them. I lost 25 of the 30 pounds rig away, only to pack it on again while nursing.

I had been on a pretty good lc streak for several months before going on vacation 4 weeks ago. I had incorporated dr kruse bab and that had really helped with cravings. I had been doing ct several times a week and that was going well. I hadn't lost any weight, but I hadn't gained any either. Then we went on vacation for 17 days. The first few days were great on the road. I had packed a cooler and all of us ate great. Then we got to my husbands fathers house and that's when the mess began. They had asked us before we came what we liked to eat etc and I told them we focused on whole foods: meat, eggs, veggies, and sometimes fruit for the kids. I told them we avoided processed stuff, sugar, and wheat. They said, great, no problem, we'll be sure to have that on hand. Nope... Breakfast first morning cinnamon rolls, then pancakes, continuing like that. Dinners of BBQ ribs, potatoes, corn, Mac and cheese, lasagna. I should have probably done something early in the visit...gone to the grocery store to get food or something. I figured I'd just get back on the program after we left, but the whole vacation ended up being a big carb fest. Came home to a 14 pound gain (ouch), though I have dropped 10 of those with being only partially back on plan.

I tried getting back on program, but I just don't seem to be able to stick to it. I'm frustrated with myself for not sticking to the diet...not having th willpower. I'm frustrated with my body for not dropping weight even when I do stick to it. I'm tired of dieting. I wish I could finally convince myself I didn't like all of the sweet treats. I tell myself how bad they are, I talk about how bad they are, I write about it, but I still enjoy eating them. I don't like feeling like I have to choose between eating healthy and having a social life. I always avoid social events with food (which is all of them) because I know I'm weak willed and might cheat if I'm around bad food.

Alright....there is part of my pathetic story. Any suggestions for getting back on track?

### indigogirl — 2012-06-13T21:46:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-30956>

Trisha,

First you are not pathetic nor weak willed!

I don't know what's driving the eating for you, but something is surely driving it.

I wish I had words of wisdom or advice. I really don't other than keep posting here, continue reading other's posts and journals, keep eating your BAB, stay on the LRx and BE KIND TO YOURSELF. There are a lot of us here going through similar things.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-13T21:47:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-30957>

aww...sorry to hear that! Why is it so hard with family? I go through the same stuff... don't beat yourself up, it's just an excuse to eat more. Focus on the BAB and fat and protein, fat and protein. The carb demons will die down eventually. Focus on where you are going not where you've been. And I hear you on the "I'm so sick of being on a diet" thing. Can I suggest changing your thinking to accepting a new way of eating for life? Find something you can live with. Make good recipes. Eat food that tastes good. Don't focus on restricting so you lose weight now and then add stuff in later. Eat now like you are gonna eat for the rest of your life and take the slower losses. You just had twins! What a blessing! You'll get there (and have you had your hormones tested?) The biggest cause of weight gain is inflammation and toxins. Eat clean, eat to decrease inflammation. Real life journies are seldom a straight path.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-14T04:08:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-31043>

Hi Trisha,

There are a lot of us who can relate to what you are going through! You can do this. You're back home and in control of your food again. The cravings are the killer. I binged last Monday after being binge-free for 77 days. Normally that would have led to at least a week long food fest but it didn't. The BAB was key. I need to eat at least 65 grams of protein with lots of fat in order to keep the cravings at bay. After my binge last week I had some cravings again but I was strict with the BAB and therefore they were "ignorable" and I'm now back on track!

Your story is not pathetic, it's just a bump on your road to success!

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-06-14T17:50:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-31128>

Thank you thank you for your words of encouragement! I was having a slight mental melt down yesterday. Feeling more clear today. Yesterday was not a good eating day, but I'm determined today will be. I just have to be sure to keep that resolution! Earlier in the day it's always harder. It seems my resolutions always wear thin as the day drags on. I know i have a few challenges that await me in the coming days too. Saturday we are having a couple of friends over and Sunday is a big family Father's Day pool party/picnic at my house. The summer pool parties are a killer for me because everyone brings lots of tasty treats to those.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-06-15T16:46:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-31330>

I had a really good day yesterday. Ate well and enjoyed holding my babies a lot. It felt like they were nursing constantly yesterday, maybe because of the low grade fever they were running?

Babies had a rough night last night. That's two nights in a row with not more than 2.5 hours sleep each night. I'm beat today. Determined to eat good again today despite the fatigue.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-15T17:18:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-31336>

Glad to hear you had a good day yesterday. I know being tired is hard. I hope you had a great BAB!!! Good luck today, you can do it!

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-06-16T21:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-31587>

Feeling a bit better today as I had almost 4 hours of sleep. One of these days maybe my babies will let me get 6 or maybe even 8 hours! It's been so long since I've had a normal nights sleep. At least a year and a half. The last half or more of my pregnancy with the twins I was so uncomfortable I wasn't sleeping much.

Yesterday was another good food day - 2 days under my belt. So far today has gone well. I have had some pretty massive carb cravings today. Feeling a bit weak and shakey, but ill survive.

I've had a busy morning with kids and getting ready for our first big pool party of the yEar tomorrow. Speaking of which, I'm really nervous about. I wish I had more good days under my belt. For some reason it's always easier for me to face social situations and bad food when I do.

Anybody care to share how you physically or mentally ready yourself for those types of challenges?

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-06-16T21:08:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-31588>

Oh, and happy news. I'm just 2 pounds away from my pre-vacation weight. I'm glad it's come off easily. Would be nice to see more come off easily, but I'm sure that won't happen. While I'm nursing these babies my weight just doesn't want to budge. I. Know with my older two when my cycle came back I saw some losses - very slow, but movement in the right direction. My cycle came back months before this point though. I'm sure it has to do with nursing two though.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-18T15:38:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-31919>

I hope your pool party was a good one. Whatever happened yesterday, just remember to eat a BAB today!

### allison09 — 2012-06-19T05:04:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-32081>

Hi Trisha!

I have a hard time with parties/social events too. I always get really anxious about the food situation and tend to dwell on it. For instance, I have to go to a rehearsal dinner on Thursday and be in a wedding on Saturday, and here I am thinking "I should just wait to do the reset rill after". Why am I going to let 2 days dictate my entire week? I'll never know. Ridiculous, I know, but true. What I've realized is that people don't pay as much attention to what I'm eating or not eating as I think they will. I usually make sure I'm holding a drink (water), or have a small plate with veggies or something on it in front of me. If people ask, I just say that I have digestive issues (true!) or that I'm full, or that I just ate or something like that. Or I'll change the subject-tends to work. As far as facing the cravings, that one can be tricky. Maybe just make sure you eat something with protein and fat beforehand, that way you're not physically hungry? Sorry I couldn't offer better advice, just know that you're stronger than some sugary treat, and that you deserve to eat REAL food!!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-19T05:15:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-32082>

i love chaos.....because when I see people eating stuff they should not be.....i go Jack attack but on the sly......once I get one party goer.....they all gravitate to learn more because the buzz is on......and this way the next party is paleo.......because they all are ambiently aware.......now. Make good out your bad and stop focusing in on the bad you see or think. Turn the carnage into a cultivated field of knowledge. Create the future from the present nightmare.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-06-22T05:45:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-32892>

I made it through the party flawlessly only to get waylaid on Monday. ARGH! Back on plan now though. I didn't let it turn into several days of a big eating disaster, just two meals. Going to keep plowing ahead.

Sure would be nice to take a few pounds off. I have a beautiful pool and backyard and we have lots of parties in the summer but this summer and last (I had just had the babies the end of may) I haven't been able to enjoy the pool when guests are here. I hate that, feel like a total party pooper.

On a positive note, we purchased half of a pastured pig and it will be delivered tomorrow. I can't wait! It's pork for dinner tomorrow.

I haven't gotten back to ct since we've been home. It's hard now that the pool has warmed up! Gotta figure out a new way to do it and get back in the groove sometime!

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-06-25T17:56:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-33461>

I'm doing pretty good. Staying the course since my blip lat Monday. I have been having my bab, and most days not eating till dinner. Some days I need a little lunch though. I've been super tired lately, not sure what that's about. Wish my babies would learn to sleep better, so I could get better sleep!

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-06-28T23:27:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-34237>

Took advantage of Dr Kruses 2 for 1 consult deal last night and had my initial consult with him. He kinda scared me, I think it's what I needed to make me get serious about my diet, and not just my diet my kids diet too. I'm doing good today. I have a picnic/birthday party tonight to go to, but I don't feel worried at all, at the moment, about cheating. I know what I can eat going into it and I have a plan... that's always good. I hope I can continue to keep the goal and focus in my vision and continue to speak truth to myself. I don't need that unhealthy food, and I know it won't even taste good to me once I develop my appetite for real healthy food.

I was very pleased today when I called my doctors office. My new doctor seems very hard to get into, but I called to find out if I could move up my apt which wasn't for another 3 weeks. They have an opening on Tuesday morning. Hoping I'll be able to go in and request the labs that Dr. Kruse suggested and not have too many problems getting them.

My big goal now: develop an appetite for seafood. I loathe seafood more than any other food. I don't know how I'm going to do this, but I've got to at least start trying to eat some seafood. If I can spice it up enough so I don't taste the fishy flavor I'll probably be alright. Any ideas?

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-28T23:37:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-34243>

I'm not a seafood fan either but this past weekend I was away and managed to eat mussels coated in butter and a lobster with loads of butter and it wasn't too bad! Other than that I'm in the same boat as you, not a fan!

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-06-29T01:05:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-34264>

> **paulalynn;34400 wrote:** I'm not a seafood fan either but this past weekend I was away and managed to eat mussels coated in butter and a lobster with loads of butter and it wasn't too bad! Other than that I'm in the same boat as you, not a fan!

I suppose you can't go too wrong when you smother most anything in butter! Maybe add some garlic too

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-06-29T23:31:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-34456>

Another great day under my belt, even with a party yesterday! I don't even feel deprived. I do think eating plenty of fat in addition to protein for my bab keeps me from feeling hungry throughout the day. And something about eating meat in the morning and not just eggs really is satisfying.

Feeling really irritated today about a number of things. One of them is that it looks like the lab neglected to run all of the labs that my doctor ordered last time and since my apt is this coming Tuesday there is no way to get them done prior to meeting with him. The labs I was particularly interested were the ones to check to see if I have hashimotos. If those are positive I have a list from dr kruse of more labs to pursue. I'm just anxious to get it all checked out and figure out what's going on.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-07-01T00:59:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-34688>

First day in a while that I've had major carb cravings. I don't think my meals today have had as much fat in them. I think they've been heavier protein. Going to make a mental note and see if this is a pattern.

We are having a pool party here tomorrow afternoon, so I'm hoping I don't have these cravings tomorrow as there will be lots of off limit foods.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-07-02T03:09:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-34923>

Yay, proud moment.... I made it though today's pool party without touching any off limits foods. There was a cake that looked and smelled so scrumptious, but I just told myself, "girl, this is NOT worth ruining your health over!"

I've been trying to move my family towards this way of eating, but I haven't yet cracked down at parties or when they're at their grandparents and whatnot, but I've been talking to my husband about starting to get more strict about that. As I've thought about it though, I know I can't ask them to do something I myself am not sticking to. Perfect reason to stick to my guns!

So today... I'm thankful for small victories, because one by one they add up to a big mountain of victories!

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-07-02T18:35:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-35045>

Argh! Frustrating morning. My three youngest are sick. The two babies are running fevers and sleeping particularly horribly. So, I had to reschedule my dr apt. Can't get in for over a month. I am so anxious to get these labs that dr kruse recommended done, it frustrates me to have to wait this long.

On top of that, I've been trying to be less obsessive about stepping on the scale. Partly because it just seems to upset me lately. I stepped on the scale this morning after 10 days off and saw a two pound weight gain. \*big sigh\* It doesn't seem to matter what I do. The weight just keeps piling on.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-07-05T18:47:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-35659>

I stopped caring what the scale says. I don't know \*how\* I stopped caring but I did. I was caring too much and it lead to my last binge. I was so frustrated I figured "what the hell...". Since that day, I'm more focused on fixing what's broken. The scale barely moves but I figure I have to be doing something right. I must be fixing things from the inside out!

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-07-13T05:25:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-37409>

I sure wish I could stop caring what the scale said too, but I just do care. Well, I guess I wouldn't care if I could fit in the clothes I want to fit into and feel great...lthen, who cares what the number is. Problem is, I'm way too fat (weigh as much as I did 13 months ago when I gave birth to my twins) and way too tired. And I can't afford to be tired as a stay at home mom of four who homeschools. When fall hits I'm going to have to be on top of stuff. I'll be teaching a high school bio class for a homeschool group (my bachelors is in biology) and might possibly be doing some counseling in the evening (thats what my masters is in). I just can't afford to be this worn out.

Eating has been going pretty good. I've had ZERO grains and sugar, but I haven't cut out dairy yet. Kinda waiting on test results for that one. I haven't always been getting my BAB in first thing in the morning. I really drag on the mornings and have a hard time getting going. It's really awfully busy getting my kids fed and cleaned up. It seems like by the time Ive got a chance to eat half the morning is gone. Often times I do eat a bit here and there in the am. I warm a piece of meat and eat bites between everything else.

Still haven't started back with CT. I actually meant to ask Dr kruse about that when we had our consult, but I forgot. Wondering if I should be doing CT and if there was anything in particular I should be aware of. I need to remember that for the next consult, but that will probably be little while.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-07-13T05:36:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-37413>

Oh, and starting in the fall I'm taking a formal Latin class. I only know what I've self taught while learning with my son. I decided I need to be ahead of him to be a good teacher so I'm taking the plunge. I need energy and my he'd to be less fuzzy if I'm going to ace this class like the over achiever that I am demands

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-07-16T20:41:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-38432>

My 8 year old cracks me up. He asks me this afternoon at lunch, why can't we eat a balanced diet? And I ask him what he means thinking i will hear some derivation of the food pyramid. Nope, he says, you know mom, we eat some good for you food and some bad for you food so it all balances out. Silly kid.

In other news... I'm working on a blog post trying to compile all of the ct or benefits of cold type of resources I can find. Mainly this will just be a resource for myself when I want to find something and remember what it's about because I think most of my friends and family who read my blog think I'm nuts when it comes to this cold stuff!

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-07-23T19:54:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-40420>

I finally have my dr apt coming up on August 2nd. I'm hoping my doc will order all of the labs dr kruse recommended. I am a bit nervous about how to approach him with a big list of labs.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-07-24T20:45:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-40767>

The labs dr kruse recommended are

Thyroid panel (my doc always runs this anyway so it shouldn't be a big deal)

Vitamin d (I know my doc is a big proponent of optimum vit d levels, so also shouldn't be a prob)

Lipid panel (haven't had one done in years, so I'm sure he will run this)

Hs-crp (no idea how to explain why I want this)

Salivary cortisol and dhea (I'm guessing insurance won't cover this, so I can order it on my own if my doc doesn't want to)

Test for celiac (I have family history of this, so I'll bet he will run this too)

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-08-03T21:40:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-42745>

Operation doctor appointment was a success! That is, I was able to (easily) get all labs that I requested on dr kruse's recommendation. I was so nervous he wasn't going to go for it, but he actually suggested a couple of the labs himself. When I suggested the others he said he thought that was a "good idea." He had a few more labs he added to the list as well.

I'm so vey pleased how the appointment went. This was only the second time I've gone to this doctor. I had high hopes for him. He works at a "preventative medicine center" and considers himself a "complimentary physician" so far, I'm very happy with my two appointments. He seems very open to suggestions which is so different to the "check box doctors" I've been to.

He also had me set up an appointment with another doctor in his practice who is an allergist and Has a fellowship in environmental medicine. I guess we will see how it goes!

Tests I'm getting

Atherotech VAP

Cardio CRP

CBC with diff

CMP IgE

Total T3

Free T3

FreeT4

TSH

B12

RBC

Folate

MMA

FE

TIBC

Ferritin

%saturation

Antigliadin and endomyseal abs

Tissue transglutaminase abs

25 hydroxy vit D

Salivary cortisol and DHEA

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2013-02-11T04:56:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gettin-my-head-in-the-game.1856/post-75357>

I haven't updated this in FOREVER. I haven't been online a lot lately, I've been uber busy with kids and homeschooling and all that fun stuff. But, a happy update. I had been following the Leptin Reset for many months with no weight loss (sadness), but it did stop me from gaining weight while nursing. My babies started drastically cutting back on nursing about 2 months ago and weaned completely about 4ish weeks ago. In the past 2 months I've seamlessly dropped 17 pounds. I know for some people that's not a dramatic weight loss, but for me, who only loses 2-3 pounds a month, it's awesome! My appetite has plummeted. The only reason I think to eat is because my kiddos want to eat. I'm feeling good. I feel like my doctor finally has my thyroid levels where they should be. I'm due for more testing in a couple of weeks, so it should be interesting to see where they are.

If anyone reads this, I just wanted to report happy news

### caroline — 2013-02-11T05:47:38+0200
